name: spy
note: spy gene expression is induced separately by both copper and zinc metals through the CpxAR and BaeSR sensor response regulatory systems, respectively. BaeR can be the primary regulator and CpxR can enhance its function |CITS:[17884222][24999585]|. On the other hand, BaeR is the primary regulator of spy in the presence of the protein-unfolding agent ethanol |CITS:[24999585]|. The expression of the gene spy is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|. spy is also induced by indole |CITS: [15686558] [12354228]|. Under conditions leading to NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the spy gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|. The spy gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: bamB-der
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for bamB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: recC
note: There is a region of dyad symmetry upstream of recC, and this may be a region to control the gene expression. Based on the bacteriostatic antibiotic-induced persister model, 37 and 9 genes were found to produce defects in rifampin- and tetracycline-induced persister formation, respectively. Only six mutants were found to overlap in both treatment-induced persister screens: recA, recC, ruvA, uvrD, fis, and acrB. Four of these mutants (recA, recC, ruvA, and uvrD) mapped to the DNA repair pathway, one mutant mapped to a global transcriptional regulator (fis), and one to a gene for an efflux protein (acrB) |CITS:[29559967]|. Other genes that mapped to transporters, membrane biogenesis, LPS biosynthesis, flagellum biosynthesis, metabolism (folate and energy), and translation were more specific to rifampin-induced persisters |CITS:[29559967]|.

name: dsbC-recJ-prfB
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for prfB and recJ . This structure could regulate the expression of the genes, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: greA
note: Potrykus et al. (2009) identified four promoters for the greA gene; however, based on two of them (called here the P3 and P4 promoters) they lack a clear consensus, they show no activity in vitro, and finally, lacZ fusion yields barely measurable activities. The P3 and P4 bands which were identified by primer extension could be generated during posttranscriptional processes |CITS:[20008510]|. greA gene expression is subject to self-repression. The autoregulatory effect is indirect, or additional factors are necessary |CITS:[20008510]|.

name: lptB-rpoN-hpf-ptsN-yhbJ
note: The expression of the rpoN gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants of two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, it is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on the gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the two promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: flhBAE
note: There is a consensus sequence for a σ28 factor at 120 bp upstream of the transcription initiation site of the flhBp promoter, which could be a second promoter for the flhB operon |CITS:[7961507]|. The DNA sequence was not shown. In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of flhB as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: fliE
note: In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of fliE as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: fliFGHIJK
note: In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of fliGHIJ as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: gltIJKL-sroC
note: The transcription of the gltI gene is repressed during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: rnlB
note: Otsuka et al. in 2010 reported that there is a terminator sequence of transcription just downstream of the rnlB gene |CITS:[ 20421606]|, but the sequence was not reported. Therefore, we have not added this terminator to the database.

name: nrdHIEF
note: nrdHIEF is induced under H2O2 stress conditions (oxidative stress) |CITS:[21338418]|. Fur, NrdR, and IscR control nrdHIEF expression independently of one another |CITS:[21338418]|. An increase in the expression of nrdH has been observed when CysB is highly overexpressed |CITS: [31823602]|.

name: cpxPQ
note: The expression of the gene cpxP is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|. Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the cpxP gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|. cpxP is positively regulated in the presence of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) through CpxR |CITS:[29497416]|. The cpxP gene is upregulated by short-term (30 min) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: srkA-dsbA
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the dsbA gene is increased |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: fkpA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for fkpA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: fkpA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for fkpA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: dmsABC
note: The regulatory effect of FlhDC in dmsABC operon transcription has been proved only by microarray analysis by |CITS: [11287152]|

name: ptsG
note: The ptsG gene appears to be regulated by FNR, but Shalel-Levanon et al. |CITS:[15988767]| reported a positive effect and Salmon et al. |CITS:[15988767]| reported a negative effect of this regulation; none of them reported which of the two promoters (ptsGp1 or ptsGp2) is affected, even is not known if this regulation is direct or indirect. On the other hand, under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the ptsG gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur |CITS:[21272324]|. ptsG expression is under heat shock stress conditions |CITS:[11340070]|. Expression of ptsG is increased severalfold during cell growth on glucose or other PTS substrates |CITS:[18402772]|. ptsG is regulated posttranscriptionally by modulation of mRNA stability in response to glycolytic flux |CITS:[11432845]| and to the availability of oxygen |CITS:[18402772]|. On the other hand, accumulation of glucose-6-phosphate induces expression of the SgrS small RNA and, subsequently, possible base paring with the 5'-UTR of the ptsG mRNA accelerates RNase E-dependent degradation of the ptsG transcript |CITS:[11432845][15522088]|. The expression of the ptsG gene is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The plasticity of the promoter regions was demonstrated for two CRP target genes, ptsG and manX . Four genetically different strains with the crp gene deleted were propagated in a glucose minimal medium. This deletion led to deleterious effects on growth in the four strains, but most of the populations quickly evolved compensatory changes that restored fast growth and were specific to the glucose environment in which they evolved. The adaptation involved plasticity of the promoter regions of two CRP target genes involved in glucose transport, ptsG and manX |CITS:[ 30825312]|.

name: ptsG
note: The ptsG gene appears to be regulated by FNR, but Shalel-Levanon et al. |CITS:[15988767]| reported a positive effect and Salmon et al. |CITS:[15988767]| reported a negative effect of this regulation; none of them reported which of the two promoters (ptsGp1 or ptsGp2) is affected, even is not known if this regulation is direct or indirect. On the other hand, under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the ptsG gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur |CITS:[21272324]|. ptsG expression is under heat shock stress conditions |CITS:[11340070]|. Expression of ptsG is increased severalfold during cell growth on glucose or other PTS substrates |CITS:[18402772]|. ptsG is regulated posttranscriptionally by modulation of mRNA stability in response to glycolytic flux |CITS:[11432845]| and to the availability of oxygen |CITS:[18402772]|. On the other hand, accumulation of glucose-6-phosphate induces expression of the SgrS small RNA and, subsequently, possible base paring with the 5'-UTR of the ptsG mRNA accelerates RNase E-dependent degradation of the ptsG transcript |CITS:[11432845][15522088]|. The expression of the gene ptsG is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: grcA
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the grcA ( yfiD ) gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene grcA is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|, but the transcription may be activated by Fnr, whose gene is also upregulated under these conditions. This increased expression of grcA is in agreement with the increased formate production observed under acidic conditions |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: ldcC
note: RpoS-dependent expression at stationary phase has been described |CITS: [9692215]|. In addition to RpoS, it appears that ldcC expression is also induced by another sigma factors, because in the presence of antibiotic and the absence of RpoS, ldcC expression is induced |CITS: [34543694]|. Fluoroquinolones and -lactams increased ldcC expression, while an aminoglycoside (amikacin) decreased such expression |CITS: [34543694]|.

name: rrsA-ileT-alaT-rrlA-rrfA
note: E. coli has seven copies of the operon that encode ribosomal RNAs (rrn operons). This multiplicity of operons is necessary so as not to saturate individual rrn operons with RNApol, which would result in transcription-replication conflicts, a longer lag phase before growth resumes, cell death, DNA damage, mutations, and SOS induction |CITS: [35552441]|. The seven ribosomal operons are complexly transcribed. Each operon has two σ70 promoters (p1 and p2); their transcription starts with a large precursor that contains rRNAs |CITS: [15686546]|; they are the target of regulation by the alarmone ppGpp, several triphosphate nucleotides (NTPs), and the transcriptional regulators FIS and HNS. FIS and HNS regulate at the p1; however, ppGpp and NTPs control both promoters in accordance with their concentration changes, even in the absence of the transcriptional regulators |CITS: [15568992] [14526030] [12504278]|. In addition, these two small regulating metabolites (ppGpp and NTPs) require the help of the DskA coregulatory protein that binds to the RNA polymerase |CITS: [15568992]|. The net amount of rRNA transcription can be seen as a complex function of the ratio of inhibitors to activators. HNS/Fis and ppGpp/NTP ratios are the highest ones during slow growth and the lowest ones during rapid growth; the ribosomal production is in accordance to the growth rate (higher in rapid growth and lower in slow growth) |CITS: [15686546] [14526030]|. It is important to note that the ribosomal gene-containing operons also encode genes whose products interact with the RNA polymerase and participate in the translation elongation and in the replication apparatus. Reviews: |CITS: [15590778] [8531889] [7517053] [2434021] [15686546] [15568992]|. Additional reference:Keener J. and Nomura M. (1996). Regulation of Ribosome Synthesis, in: Neidhardt, F. (Editor in Chief) et al., E. coli and Salmonella: Cellular and Molecular Biology. ASM Press, Washington, D.C., p.1417-1431.

name: rrsB-gltT-rrlB-rrfB
note: E. coli has seven copies of the operon that encode ribosomal RNAs (rrn operons). This multiplicity of operons is necessary so as not to saturate individual rrn operons with RNApol, which would result in transcription-replication conflicts, a longer lag phase before growth resumes, cell death, DNA damage, mutations, and SOS induction |CITS: [35552441]|. The seven ribosomal operons of E. coli are complexly transcribed. Each operon has two σ70 promoters (p1 and p2); their transcription starts with a large precursor that contains rRNAs |CITS: [15686546]|; they are the target of regulation by the alarmone ppGpp, several triphosphate nucleotides (NTPs), and the transcriptional regulators FIS and HNS. FIS and HNS regulate at the p1; however, ppGpp and NTPs control both promoters in accordance to their concentration changes, even in the absence of the transcriptional regulators |CITS: [15568992] [14526030] [12504278]|. In addition, these two small regulating metabolites (ppGpp and NTPs) require the help of the DskA coregulatory protein that binds to the RNA polymerase |CITS: [15568992]|. The transcription elongation of the operon rrsB-gltT-rrlB-rrfB is diminished by low temperatures, but this elongation is not affected by nutrient limitation or by chloramphenicol treatment |CITS: [32840734]|. The net amount of rRNA transcription can be seen as a complex function of the ratio of inhibitors to activators. HNS/Fis and ppGpp/NTP ratios are the highest ones during slow growth and the lowest ones during rapid growth; the ribosomal production is in accordance with the growth rate (higher in rapid growth and lower in slow growth) |CITS: [15686546] [14526030]|. It is important to note that the ribosomal gene-containing operons also contain genes whose products interact with the RNA polymerase and participate in the translation elongation and in the replication apparatus. Under alkylation stress, the protein AidB binds upstream of the rrnBp1 promoter, increasing the activity of this promoter |CITS:[ 21788159]|. The roles of AidB, in the regulatory region of the rrnBp1 promoter and in other promoters also containing a UP element, are to prevent and to repair the DNA damage caused by alkylating agents and counteract the block to transcription that results upon exposure to the mentioned agents |CITS:[ 21788159]|. The secondary channel factors (SCFs) GreB and DksA both selectively repress ribosomal RNA (rRNA) transcription by a similar mechanism, because their short-lived RNAP complexes do not allow sufficient time for SCFs to dissociate. Using GreB as a model to investigate the mechanism(s) of SCF-RNAP binding and action at rrsBp1, it was revealed that GreB functions by a delayed inhibition mechanism, and the results implied that DksA functions by the same mechanism |CITS:[30720429]|. Reviews: |CITS: [15590778] [8531889] [7517053] [2434021] [15686546] [15568992]|. Additional reference:Keener J. and Nomura M. (1996). Regulation of Ribosome Synthesis, in: Neidhardt, F. (Editor in Chief) et al., Escherichia coli and Salmonella: Cellular and Molecular Biology. ASM Press, Washington, D.C., p. 1417-1431.

name: rrsD-ileU-alaU-rrlD-rrfD-thrV-rrfF
note: E. coli has seven copies of the operon that encode ribosomal RNAs (rrn operons). This multiplicity of operons is necessary so as not to saturate individual rrn operons with RNApol, which would result in transcription-replication conflicts, a longer lag phase before growth resumes, cell death, DNA damage, mutations, and SOS induction |CITS: [35552441]|. The seven ribosomal operons are complexly transcribed. Each operon has two σ70 promoters (p1 and p2); their transcription starts with a large precursor that contains rRNAs |CITS: [15686546]|; they are the target of regulation by the alarmone ppGpp, several triphosphate nucleotides (NTPs), and the transcriptional regulators FIS and HNS. FIS and HNS regulate at the p1; however, ppGpp and NTPs control both promoters in accordance with their concentration changes, even in the absence of the transcriptional regulators |CITS: [15568992] [14526030] [12504278]|. In addition, these two small regulating metabolites (ppGpp and NTPs) require the help of the DskA coregulatory protein that binds to the RNA polymerase |CITS: [15568992]|. The net amount of rRNA transcription can be seen as a complex function of the ratio of inhibitors to activators. HNS/Fis and ppGpp/NTP ratios are the highest ones during slow growth and the lowest ones during rapid growth; and the ribosomal production is in accordance to the growth rate (higher in rapid growth and lower in slow growth) |CITS: [15686546] [14526030]|. It is important to note that the ribosomal gene-containing operons also encode genes whose products interact with the RNA polymerase and participate in the translation elongation and in the replication apparatus. Reviews: |CITS: [15590778] [8531889] [7517053] [2434021] [15686546] [15568992]|. Additional reference:Keener J. and Nomura M. (1996). Regulation of Ribosome Synthesis, in: Neidhardt, F. (Editor in Chief) et al., Escherichia coli and Salmonella: Cellular and Molecular Biology. ASM Press, Washington, D.C., p. 1417-1431.

(Editor in Chief) et al., Escherichia coli and Salmonella: Cellular and Molecular Biology. ASM Press, Washington, D.C., p. 1417-1431.

name: uspB
note: uspB gene expression is upregulated upon L-valine addition (isoleucine starvation) in the cellular growth medium |CITS: [32211022]|.

name: gdhA
note: Under nitrogen-limiting growth conditions, the transcription of the gdhA gene is repressed by the Nac protein |CITS: [9785451]|. Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the gdhA gene was decreased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, the gene expression was increased in the Fur mutant |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: yjjY
note: The mRNA produced by the yjjY gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: yaaX
note: The expression of the gene yaaX is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: yaaA
note: The yaaA gene is induced by H2O2 and is part of the OxyR regulon |CITS: [21378183]|.

name: insL1
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds in the intergenic region of the genes mokC and insL1 |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: mokC-hokC
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds in the intergenic region of the genes mokC and insL1 |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: yabI
note: The transcript of yabI is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: sgrR-sroA-thiBPQ
note: SgrR, which has a putative DNA-binding domain (at the amino region) and a solute-binding domain (at the carboxy region), can simultaneously regulate both sgrS activation and its autoregulation |CITS:[17209026]|, under glucose phosphate accumulation (phosphosugar stress). This SgrS synthesis causes the degradation of ptsG mRNA |CITS:[15522088]|.

name: sgrST-setA
note: The transcription of this transcriptional unit is induced under glucose-phosphate stress |CITS:[20971900]|. CRP and KdgR indirectly regulate the transcription of this operon under stress conditions, but in the absence of stress CRP represses the transcription of setA but not the transcription of the other genes of the operon, suggesting the presence of a promoter upstream of setA |CITS:[20971900]|. SgrR has a putative DNA-binding domain (at the amino region) and a solute-binding domain (at the carboxy region) and can simultaneously regulate both sgrS activation and its autoregulation |CITS:[17209026]|. This SgrS synthesis causes the degradation of ptsG mRNA |CITS:[15522088]|.

name: leuO
note: The leuO gene is one of the genes naturally regulated by the gene silencer. It is, together with the ilvIH and leuABCD operons, regulated via the promoter-relay mechanism. It was demonstrated by primer extension experiments that ilvIH promoter activity is required for activation of the leuO gene via this mechanism. The AT-rich DNA regions flanking leuO are named locus control regions LCR-I and LCR-II, respectively. LCR-I (gene silencer) corresponds to the H-NS (cis-element with little DNA sequence specificity), and LCR-II-binding sites regulate negatively and positively the leuO gene expression |CITS: [15582999]|. Expression of the leuO gene is coupled to the expression of the ilvIH operon located downstream of the leuO gene |CITS: [18055596]|. LrhA binds to a region which overlaps with the HNS-binding site and one of the LeuO-binding sites, activating the promoters leuOp1, leuOp, and leuOp3 |CITS:[28252809]|. The transcription of the leuO gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: cra
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the fruR gene was decreased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and increased in a mutant for the transcriptional regulator Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, the gene expression was increased in the cyoA and cydB mutants. It is unknown if the effects of Fur act directly on the gene expression |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene cra is decreased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The decreased expression under aerobiosis appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB and also could be caused by the increased glucose concentration observed under acidic conditions |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: nadC
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for nadC . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: map-glnD-dapD
note: Activation of the SoxS and Rob regulators of the map-glnD-dapD operon was observed after treatment with paraquat and dipyridyl |CITS: [12067348]|, and activation by MarA was observed after treatment with sodium salicylate |CITS: [11395452][18000553]|.

name: dxr-uppS-cdsA
note: The transcription unit drx-uppS-cdsA is transiently induced at low temperature |CITS: [31127859]|.

name: fur
note: The expression of the fur gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutant for the transcriptional regulator Fnr. However, it is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulator act directly on gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the four promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulator |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: yafDE
note: By using ChIP-Seq analysis, it was observed that the protein PdeL binds in the intergenic region of yafC and yafD |CITS: [34903045]|.

name: gmhA
note: The transcription of the gmhA gene appears to be positively regulated by the protein BglG |CITS: [35397010]|.

name: dinJ-yafQ
note: The dinJ gene is repressed by both DnaA and LexA in an independent manner, but the actions of these two proteins are additive during the SOS response |CITS:[29967594]|. The sites position for both regulators were not shown |CITS:[29967594]|.

name: frsA
note: The -35 region of the frsA gene overlaps with the gpt terminator. Here, RNA polymerase could be controlling the positive or negative effect on frsA gene transcription |CITS: [6397401]|.

name: yagEF
note: Repression of the yagEF operon is repressed during the exponential growth phase and derepressed in stationary phase |CITS:[29087459]|. The yagEF operon is induced by D-xylonate |CITS:[31482281]|.

name: ecpR
note: Based on cells with plasmids and three inducible promoters of genes (matA, safA, and chbB), three novel fluorescent biosensors sensitive to terahertz (THz) radiation were developed, and they controlled the expression of a fluorescent protein |CITS:[33014613]|. The induction of these biosensors was manifested as fluorescent luminosity and reflected the activity of transcription units matA, safA-ydeO, and chbBCARFG, which contain the transcription factors MatA, YdeO, and ChbR, respectively |CITS:[33014613]|.

name: ykgEFG
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ykgF. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: frmRAB
note: FrmR was identified as a transcriptional repressor for the frmRAB operon in the absence of formaldehyde under anaerobic conditions, allowing the expression of a formaldehyde detoxification system |CITS: [27934966]|.

name: hemB
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for hemB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: iraP
note: iraP gene expression is upregulated upon L-valine addition (isoleucine starvation) in the cellular growth medium |CITS: [32211022]|. J. Oberto in 2010 identified a possible binding site for NagC, AATAATTACCCACACAAAATATA, in the intergenic region of the divergent genes iraP and ddlA. This demonstration was based on different statistical methods |CITS:[21070640]|, but it is not known if NagC regulates transcription in both directions.

name: sbcDC
note: The transcription of the sbcDC operon is indirectly induced by the action of RpoS; this in some way suggests the presence of another regulatory system |CITS:[17644583]|.

name: brnQ-proY
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for brnQ . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: pdeB
note: The transcription of ylaB appears to be directed by σ38 |CITS:[19332833]|.

name: apt
note: The leader sequence upstream of the apt gene may form three alternative secondary structures |CITS:[3527873]|.

name: hemH
note: The transcription of the hemH gene is repressed during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: ppiB-lpxH
note: The transcription of the lpxH gene appears to be positively regulated by the protein BglG |CITS: [35397010]|.

name: folD-ybcJ
note: Putative promoter islands, which are regions of DNA containing multiple transcription start sites, were found by computational prediction methods in the intergenic region of the folD and sfmA genes in E. coli. These promoter islands are associated with genes acquired horizontally, and they are absent in the same region in Salmonella |CITS:[23717391]|. In addition, some sites that bind HNS have been predicted in this intergenic region. This protein appears to inactivate the promoter islands |CITS:[23717391]|. The transcription of the ybcJ gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: sfmA
note: Putative promoter islands, which are regions of DNA containing multiple transcription start sites, were found by computational prediction methods in the intergenic region of the folD and sfmA genes in E. coli. These promoter islands are associated with genes acquired horizontally, and they are absent in the same region in Salmonella |CITS:[23717391]|. In addition, some sites that bind HNS have been predicted in this intergenic region. This protein appears to inactivate the promoter islands |CITS:[23717391]|.

name: sfmHF
note: Although a putative σ54-binding site was identified upstream of the sfmF gene and the gene expression was affected in microarray experiments in a mutant σ54 strain and a σ54-overexpressing strain, ChIP assays demonstrated that σ54 does not bind upstream of sfmF |CITS:[19969540]|. Therefore, we did not assign a σ54 promoter to this gene.

name: ybdG
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ybdG. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: ybdO
note: A gene expression analysis demonstrated that the ybdO gene is negatively regulated by the transcriptional factor HNS |CITS:[26789284]|. Two specific regions appear to be involved in that regulation; one of the them is located upstream of the gene start site (from -250 to -176) and the other one is located downstream of the gene start site (from +27 to +164) |CITS:[26789284]|. Another region was identified that reduces the transcription of ybdO in an HNS-independent way |CITS:[26789284]|. The sequence in this region containing an HNS-independent negative element is different in strains other than E. coli K-12 |CITS:[26789284]|.

Expression of dsbG is activated by OxyR. This is also true for the expression of the divergently transcribed ahpC. The intergenic region between dsbG and ahpC carries two binding sites for OxyR, a dsbG-proximal site, located 54 bp upstream of the dsbG start codon, and an ahpC-proximal site, located 290 bp upstream of the dsbG start codon |CITS:[11443092]|. Northern blotting and primer extension assays showed that OxyR binding to the ahpC-proximal site leads to the induction of both dsbG and ahpC transcripts, while OxyR binding to the dsbG-proximal site leads to the induction of a second ahpC transcript. This transcript of ahpC and the transcript of dsbG overlap by over 100 nucleotides |CITS:[11443092]|.

name: citCDEFXG
note: Transcription of the citCDEFXG operon is dependent on CitB when E. coli is grown in the presence of citrate and the absence of nitrate and oxygen |CITS: [22101843]|. Yamamoto et al. in 2008 showed that CitB (DpiA), in the presence of citrate, activates the transcription of three promoters, citCp2, citCp1, and citAp (dpiBp), in the intergenic region located between the citC and citA genes (dpiB) |CITS: [18997424]|. But they have not shown the mechanism of action of CitB in the activation of the divergent operons.

name: dpiBA
note: The genes of the dpiBA operon codes for proteins of two-component regulatory system, the sensor kinase (DpiB) and the response regulator (DpiA). These proteins induce the SOS response |CITS: [15308764]|. Transcription of the citCDEFXG operon is dependent on CitB when E. coli is grown in the presence of citrate and the absence of nitrate and oxygen |CITS: [22101843]|. Yamamoto et al. in 2008 showed that CitB (DpiA), in the presence of citrate, activates the transcription of three promoters, citCp2, citCp1, and citAp (dpiBp), in the intergenic region located between the citC and citA genes (dpiB) |CITS: [18997424]|. But they have not shown the mechanism of action of CitB in the activation of the divergent operons.

name: cspE
note: The cspE gene is constitutively expressed at 37°C and is induced about threefold at both the transcript and the protein levels in response to cold shock. cspE is also regulated at the posttranscriptional level by RNAse E and temperature-dependent UTR secondary structures |CITS: [18177308]|. Previously, cspE was shown to be involved in the regulation of the expression of some cold-induced genes |CITS: [16547039]|; in particular, it might play an important role in the early stages of cold adaptation |CITS: [18177308]|. The mRNA produced by the cspE gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: lipA
note: PdhR was identified by Kaleta et al. (2010) as a negative regulator of lipA, based on consensus sequences, microarrays, and electromobility shift analyses (EMSA) at bp 69 (designated by Feng and Cronan [2014] as lipA site 1) of the lipAp promoter |CITS:[20718955]|. However, Feng and Cronan (2014) reported that this site is nonfunctional and physiologically irrelevant. The binding detected by Kaleta et al. (2010) is due to another site (lipA site 3) that is located far upstream of the lipAp promoter and which is a half-palindrome with weak PdhR-binding ability |CITS:[24816490]|. Other possible PdhR-binding degenerate palindromes were identified by Feng and Cronan (2014). One of them is downstream of the lipAp promoter at positions 22 to 38 (lipA site 2) and the other one is at positions -293 to -277 (lipA site 3). All three of these sites were present in the EMSA probe used by Kaleta et al. (2010) and thus could contribute to the weak binding observed |CITS:[24816490]|. On the other hand, deletion of pdhR did not cause any detectable change in the lipA transcription level for β-galactosidase activity, indicating that PdhR plays no role in regulation of lipA expression |CITS:[24816490]|. It was concluded that lipA is not controlled by PdhR, based on in vivo and in vitro evidence |CITS:[24816490]|.

name: rsfS-rlmH-mrdAB-rlpA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for mrdB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: leuS-lptE-holA-nadD-cobC
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for leuS . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: rihA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rihA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: chiZPQ
note: Rasmussen et. al., (2009) identified a ybfM-specific RNA species; however, it may be generated by premature termination in the ybfM-N intergenvic region or by degradation of the full-length transcript |CITS:[19400782]|. Regions protected by GalR and GalS upstream of the chiPp promoter were identified by DNase I footprinting |CITS:[24593230]|. A binding site for both regulators was identified, 167.5 bp upstream of the chiPp promoter. In addition, two other weak binding sites for GalS nearer to the chiPp promoter were also identified |CITS:[24593230]|. However, mutation in neither galR nor galS had any effect on chiP-lacZ expression |CITS:[24593230]|.

name: ybhP-clsB-ybhN
note: The expression of an antisense RNA that starts downstream of the ybhN gene is inhibited by RNA polymerase-binding RNA aptamers (RAPs) and the protein Rho |CITS: [31535128]|.

name: cecR-ybhGFSR
note: The ybhG gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: mcbA
note: The mcbA gene was significantly upregulated in bacterial cells associated with lettuce roots in a microarray analysis |CITS: [22830299]|.

name: bssR
note: The bssR gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: rybB
note: The expression of the rybB gene is enhanced during the transition from the exponential to the stationary phase |CITS:[17007876]|, but this induction is RpoS independent |CITS:[17416652]|. The NsrR transcriptional repressor regulates negatively the transcription of the rybB gene, but it is not known if this effect is direct or indirect |CITS:[17416652]|. The RNA produced by the rybB gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell during stress conditions |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: ycaD
note: The transcription of the ycaD gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: pflA
note: The expression of the gene pflA is increased under acidic growth conditions in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression of this gene is in agreement with the increased formate production observed under acidic conditions |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: ycaO
note: Based on the improved computational program DTI (Direct Information criterion), which is an extension of the concept of MI that allows investigators to measure the direction of information flow between two random variables, and DTInfer, a new R-package for the inference of gene regulatory networks from microarrays based on directed information, the ycaO gene was identified as a new target of Fis |CITS:[20718955]|.

name: ldtD
note: Analysis of gene expression has proved that under NlpE lipoprotein overexpression, the presence of the protein CpxR causes increased transcription of the ycbB gene |CITS:[ 23564175]|. ldtD transcription is upregulated in the presence of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) through CpxR |CITS:[29497416]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ldtD . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: asnS
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for asnS . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: yccA
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the yccA gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for yccA. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: gfcA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for gfcA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: wrbA-yccJ
note: The wrbA gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: efeU
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the efeU gene is decreased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: ghrA
note: Based on DNA microarray analysis and Fourier transform-infrared spectroscopy, an Escherichia coli strain was exposed to 10 adverse conditions. Expression of three genes, ygaW, rmf, and ghrA, was significantly changed under each of these conditions (except in the presence of the disinfectant benzalkonium chloride) |CITS:[19767843]|.

name: ycdZ
note: The functions of the CRP and CytR transcriptional regulator (CRP-CytR-CRP system) were added based on the behavior of this system in Escherichia coli |CITS:[23028500]|.

name: ymdA
note: The activity of the ymdA promoter was increased after cold shock exposure |CITS:[ 28582517]|. The ymdA transcript is present during the exponential growth phase |CITS:[ 28582517]|. The probable ymdAB-clsC polycistronic transcript was detected only after cold shock exposure |CITS:[ 28582517]|. Overexpression of ymdA inhibited biofilm formation and increased the susceptibility to apramycin. These effects were dependent on the presence of the sucA gene. On the other hand, biofilm formation increased in ymdA knockout cells compared with wild-type cells. This effect was even greater in ymdA-ymdB-clsC triple knockout cells |CITS:[ 28582517]|.

name: ymdB-clsC
note: ymdB and clsC are transcribed together, and YmdB and ClsC may function in the same biological pathway |CITS:[28582517]|. Both genes are induced during the stationary phase |CITS:[28582517]|.

name: mdtG
note: mdtG gene expression is positively controlled by MarA and SoxS |CITS:[21097628]|.

name: yceJI
note: Analysis of gene expression has proved that under NlpE lipoprotein overexpression, the presence of the protein CpxR causes increased transcription of the yceJ gene; however, no DNA sequence related to the consensus DNA-binding site of CpxR has been identified in the regulatory region of the gene. Therefore, the effect of CpxR on yceJ is probably indirect |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: mdtH
note: mdtH expression is increased at least 5-fold by indole |CITS: [15686558]|.

name: ycfJ
note: Based on the improved computational program DTI (Direct Information criterion), which is an extension of the concept of MI that allows investigators to measure the direction of information flow between two random variables, and DTInfer, a new R-package for the inference of gene regulatory networks from microarrays based on directed information, the ycfJ gene was identified as a new target of PdhR |CITS:[20718955]|. The expression of the gene ycfJ is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: icdC
note: In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicates persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid completely abolished the expression of the icdC gene |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: ymgGD
note: The expression of this operon is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: nhaB
note: Analysis of gene expression has proved that under NlpE lipoprotein overexpression, the presence of the protein CpxR causes decreased transcription of the nhaB gene; however, no DNA sequence related to the consensus DNA-binding site of CpxR has been identified in the regulatory region of the gene. Therefore, the effect of CpxR on nhaB is probably indirect |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: ychS
note: ychS is preferentially transcribed by Eσ70 in vivo |CITS:[21398637]|.

name: purU
note: Based on gene expression analysis, it was shown that CsgD could positively regulate purU expression |CITS:[23824318]|.

name: yciGFE
note: The yciGFE operon is poorly expressed |CITS:[20713450]|.

name: yciK-btuR
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for btuR . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: lapAB
note: The lapAB operon is subject to heat shock |CITS:[24722986]|.

name: puuAP
note: The transcription of puuA is induced by putrescine and overexpressed by upon deletion of puuR |CITS: [18495664]|. All puu genes, including puuA and puuD, may could be synchronistically regulated by PuuR and/or other regulators, such as ArcA and FNR, which repressed the expression of puuA under anaerobic conditions |CITS: [18495664]|. Putrescine is catabolized by products of the puu gene cluster, which that are regulated by ArcA, FNR, and PuuR to decrease the toxicity of putrescine and to utilize putrescine as both a nitrogen and carbon sources |CITS: [18495664]|. The puuA gene is downregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|. The curator has determined that the binding sites of PuuR may have a length of 20 nucleotides with an inverted repeated symmetry (ATGGaCAATATATTGaCCAT). The sequence proposed by Nemoto et al. in 2012 was included in these regions |CITS: [22522900]|. Nemoto et al. proposed binding sites that are 15 nucleotides long, with an asymmetric recognition sequence, AAAATATAATGAACA |CITS: [22522900]|.

name: puuDRCBE
note: PuuR repress the expression of puuD |CITS:[15590624]|. The expression of puuD is induced by putrescine and O2; and reduced by succinate or NH4Cl |CITS:[16499623]|. The expression of puuD is repressed at the exponential phase |CITS:[16499623]| and in an anaerobic environment |CITS:[15659690]|,; and it is induced at the stationary phase |CITS:[16499623]|. The curator has determined that the binding sites of PuuR may have a length of 20 nucleotides with an inverted repeated symmetry (ATGGaCAATATATTGaCCAT). The sequence proposed by Nemoto et al. in 2012 was included in these regions |CITS:[22522900]|. Nemoto et al. proposed binding sites that are 15 nucleotides long, with an asymmetric recognition sequence, AAAATATAATGAACA |CITS:[22522900]|. A novel strain that overexpresses puuC under the control of a strong promoter led to lower rates of glycerol accumulation and higher 3-hydroxypropionic acid (3-HP) production. The PuuR transcription factor was also inactivated, producing the same results. Combined, these strategies further improved 3-HP yield with a concomitant reduction in glycerol accumulation, and the engineered strain, JHS_Δgypr-PT7, produced 53.7 g/L 3-HP and 1.5 g/L glycerol |CITS:[31864087]|.

name: ycjMNOPQRSTUV-ymjB-ompG
note: The computational prediction for this TU does not contain the ompG gene, but the coregulation analysis indicates that it does |CITS: [31253770]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ycjS. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: uspE
note: uspE gene expression is induced under exposure to hydrogen peroxide |CITS:[20209085]|.

name: abgABT-ogt
note: Although a putative σ54-binding site was identified upstream of the abgB gene and the gene expression was affected in microarray experiments in a mutant σ54 strain and a σ54-overexpressing strain, ChIP assays demonstrated that σ54 does not bind upstream of abgB |CITS:[19969540]|. Therefore, we did not assign a σ54 promoter to this gene.

name: ttcA
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of ttcA |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: ydaSTUVW-rzpR
note: Both YdaS and YdaT are toxic to cell and its overexpression reduces growth |CITS:[29205228]|. Expression of ydaS is kept silent under normal physiological conditions |CITS:[29205228]|.

name: uspF
note: The uspF gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: ompN
note: Expression levels of both micC and ompN are increased in the presence of -lactam antibiotics. On the other hand, under conditions when cells were depleted of σE upon overexpression of its cognate anti-sigma RseA, the activities of both micC and the ompN-lacZ fusion were affected. However, the σE core promoter motif was not found, which suggests an indirect effect |CITS:[29211019]|. Finally, based on gene expression analysis, it was shown that H-NS could be regulating the expression of micC and ompN |CITS:[29211019]|. MicC sRNA could be acting together with σE to control OmpC/N levels in response to -lactam antibiotics |CITS:[29211019]|. The transcription of the ompN gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: hslJ
note: The expression of the gene ydeI is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: ortT
note: Based on ChIP-chip and consensus sequences (Virtual Footprint), a site of NsrR negatively controlling the ydcX gene has been identified |CITS:[19656291]|. The consensus sequence found for this gene is AAGATGGATAAG, which lies in the intergenic region yncL-ydcX.

name: mcbR
note: Based on nickel enrichment DNA microarray, electrophoretic mobility shift assay (EMSA), and quantification of mcbR promoter activity, it was shown that YgiV represses mcbR expression |CITS:[18309357]|. The yncC gene of Salmonella enterica serovar Typhimurium shows regulation that is RpoS dependent and growth phase dependent |CITS: [11004173]|.

name: yddG
note: The expression of the yddG gene is activated on medium with NaCl and with L-phenylalanine |CITS:[19534419]|.

name: adhP
note: A possible role in the rpsA-ihfB, adhP, fecIR, and fecABCDE operons was described for functional abortive RNAs (faRNAs), based on computational prediction and described by Nair et al. 2017 |CITS:[28347827]|.

name: ydeN
note: J. Oberto in 2010 identified two possible binding sites for NagC in the upstream region of the ydeN gene (CTTATTTCTCTTCGTAAAATTAC and GTTGTTTATCGGCGAGAAATTAC). This demonstration was based on different statistical methods |CITS:[21070640]|.

name: lsrK
note: The transcription of the lsrK gene is repressed after 24 h of coculture with microcapsules of Lactobacillus rhamnosus |CITS: [31147809]|.

name: dgcZ
note: Base on a σS-deficient rpoS mutant background, ydeH is under the significant negative control of rpoS |CITS:[19332833]|. This negative control is most likely due to increased formation of a vegetative or other alternative RNAP holoenzyme in the absence of competition of σS with the other sigma factors |CITS:[19332833]|. This suggests that σS might control ydeH expression indirectly. The expression of the gene ydeH is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|. Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the ydeH gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: dicB-ydfDE-insD-intQ
note: The dicB gene of this operon (dicB-ydfDE-insD-intD) is clearly involved in the replication process |CITS: [3041373][3911021]|, but for the other genes further studies are needed to prove their participation in the same replication process |CITS: [3041373]|. This operon expression is coupled to the replication by the DicA and DicC regulatory effects |CITS: [3911021][6343351]|.

name: ynfP
note: The transcription of the ynfP gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: ynfD
note: Analysis of gene expression has proved that under NlpE lipoprotein overexpression, the presence of the protein CpxR causes increased transcription of the ynfD gene; however, no DNA sequence related to the consensus DNA-binding site of CpxR has been identified in the regulatory region of the gene. Therefore, the effect of CpxR on ynfD is probably indirect |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: add
note: The site for the RbsR transcriptional regulator in the add operon was identified based on genomic Selex analysis |CITS:[23651393]|.

name: mliC
note: The expression of the gene mliC is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: ydhR
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ydhR. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: rprA
note: The expression of the gene rprA is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|. RcsB regulates rprA transcription by both phosphorylation and acetylation. RcsBs phosphorylation and control of rprA transcription requires acetyl-phosphate (AcP) as the group donor. RcsB isolated from ackA or cob mutants is hyperacetylated, and this hyperacetylation inhibits RcsB activity through the acetylation of Lys-154, which plays an important role in rprA regulation |CITS:[23852870]|. This is the first report that in vivo acetylation by multiple lysines of a bacterial transcription factor regulates its activity |CITS:[23852870]|. rprA is induced under biofilm conditions |CITS:[ 30131361]|.

name: ydiV
note: The EAL domain protein encoded by ydiV is involved in the interaction between quorum-sensing system 1 (QS system 1) and QS system 2. QS system 1 consists of SdiA responding to signals generated by other microbial species, and QS system 2 is stimulated by the cyclic AMP (cAMP)-cAMP receptor protein (CRP protein) |CITS:[18560382]|. Expression of sdiA and ydiV is inhibited by glucose. The effect of glucose on ydiV expression is not mediated by SdiA |CITS:[18560382]|. ydiV is also induced under iron-deficient conditions in a Fur-independent way |CITS:[32813023]|.

name: tcyP
note: tcyP expression is activated in sulfate-containing medium and repressed in cysteine-containing medium |CITS:[26350134]|.

name: dgcJ
note: Based on glucose system and gene expression analyses, it was determined that both CRP and Fis activate dgcJ expression |CITS:[29196655]|.

name: yeaR-yoaG
note: Derrick et al. (2009) proposed that under conditions where Fis levels are raised, Fis binds at the yeaR promoter and prevents nitrate-dependent induction by blocking NarL binding to its target |CITS:[19245365]|. NarL activates the yeaR promoter independently of FNR |CITS:[17720788 ][19245365]|. Based on an EMSA experiment, it was shown that purified FNR has no specific DNA site for FNR on the yeaR promoter |CITS:[19245365]|. Based on experiments utilizing a mutant background, it was shown that NarP plays little or no role in the yeaR promoter region |CITS:[19245365]|.

name: pdeD
note: The transcription of yoaD appears to be directed by σ38 |CITS:[19332833]|.

name: argP
note: In 1999 Celis proposed that ArgP binds to a region of 15 bp (GTCATAGTGCAGGAA) in the intergenic region of argP |CITS: [10600368]|. In contrast, the curator identified two contiguous conserved regions, an inverted repeat of 20 bp which may be the target sequence of ArgP, located at -36.5 (TATATGCAACCTGACACAAA) and -16.5 (ATTGTGTCATAGTGCAGGAA). The sequence proposed by Celis is included in the binding site located at -16.5. The curator identified the consensus sequence of ArgP and assigned the possible central position of these binding sites, based on similarity to consensus sequences. ArgP has been identified as binding as a dimer of dimers in the intergenic region of dnaA in E. coli |CITS: [9254708]| and also in the regulatory region of gdhA of Klebsiella pneumoniae |CITS: [18424527]|. For this reason, there is a possibility that this regulator transcriptionally regulates itself as a dimer of dimers.

name: mntP
note: The reciprocal expression of mntP and mntS suggests negative regulation of mntP by the mntS transcript |CITS:[21908668]|.

name: yobF-cspC
note: The mRNA that contains the cspC gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: yebO
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for yebO. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: letAB
note: The yebST operon is related to the Mla transport pathway and is important for membrane integrity |CITS: [27795327]|.

name: cbl
note: The transcript of cbl is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: yebE
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the yebE gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: yebF
note: In conjunction with gene expression analysis, it can be argued that the yebF and yebG genes form an operon regulated by LexA |CITS:[20718955]|. Based on microarray and RT-qPCR analyses, it was determined that the yebF and nmpC genes are up- and downregulated, respectively, upon exposure to glutathione (GSH) |CITS:[29468195]|.

name: pykA
note: The expression of the gene pykA is enhanced under oxygen limitation |CITS: [30810277]|.

name: lpxM
note: The transcript of lpxM is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: ftnA
note: Fur antagonizes the H-NS effect in the ftnA promoter. Fur reverses H-NS-mediated repression of ftnA; displacement of H-NS from sites located at -131.5, -83, and -56 prevents cooperative binding at sites -20.5 and +15.5 and leads to derepression |CITS:[20015147]|. H-NS represses the ftnA operon through six AT-rich binding sites that oligomerize and cause DNA looping |CITS:[20015147]|. The displacement of the upstream sites of H-NS by Fur prevents cooperative H-NS binding at the downstream sites on the transcriptional start site of the ftnA promoter, which allows access to RNA polymerase |CITS:[20015147]|. This manner of activation by Fe2+-Fur through H-NS antisilencing represents a new mechanism for iron-induced gene expression. In addition, ftnA is the first example of a gene with a defined alternative Fe2+-Fur induction pathway in Escherichia coli |CITS:[20015147]|. Hepcidin, an antimicrobial peptide, increases the expression of ftnA under conditions of iron availability, in a Fur-independent way |CITS:[29659799]|. Expression of ftnA, along with that of other genes also regulated by Fur, is induced under Zn excess |CITS: [31586033] |.

name: sdiA
note: Expression of sdiA is inhibited by glucose |CITS:[18560382]|. Both sdiA and rcsA are involved in acyl-homoserine lactone (AHL)-mediated induction of the prophage process, and this induction is RecA independent |CITS:[19783745]|. Based on analysis with mutants, sdiA does not have any direct negative or positive regulatory role in rcsA expression, and the data suggest that intermediate transducers are involved in carrying the AHL-SdiA-mediated signal to activate rcsA for prophage induction |CITS:[19783745]|.

name: yedL
note: The transcription of the yedL gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: dgcQ
note: The transcription of yedQ appears to be directed by σ38 |CITS:[19332833]|.

name: hiuH-msrPQ
note: Transcription of the yedX (hiuH) gene is induced by high concentrations of either copper ion or hydrogen peroxide |CITS:[25568260]|. HprR activates the yedX gene in the presence of H2O2 |CITS: [27983483]|. The transcription unit hiuH-msrPQ was determined by the observation of the transcription of the intergenic regions with a part of the adjacent genes in |CITS: [34898261]|.

name: wcaCDEF-gmd-fcl-gmm-wcaI-cpsBG-wcaJ-wzxC
note: The transcription of the genes cpsB and cpsG is enhanced in the absence of cardiolipin , an anionic phospholipid that appears to participate in signaling for biofilm formation |CITS: [30782633]|. The other genes of the operon were not included in the expression analysis when cardiolipin was not present.

name: sibA
note: Based on Northern and 5'-3' RACE analysis, the sibA gene produces two distinct transcripts. The 3' end shorter transcript could be a result of processing |CITS:[18710431]|.

name: sibB
note: Based on Northern and 5'-3' RACE analysis, the sibB gene produces two distinct transcripts. The 3' end shorter transcript could be a result of processing |CITS:[18710431]|.

name: cyaR
note: A stem-loop of the ρ-independent terminator for the cyaR gene was predicted by Johansen et al. (2008). However, the terminator is inside of the cyaR gene |CITS:[18619465]|. CRP controls cyaR expression under conditions in which cAMP levels are high |CITS:[18978044]|.

name: rcnR
note: In response to increasing concentrations of nickel or cobalt, RcnR releases the repression of rcnA and rcnR to allow its expression |CITS:[16956381][19703465]|. It has been recently determined that the expression levels of the rcnA and rcnR genes are directly induced by Ni and Co via the metallo-regulator RcnR, and both genes are differentially expressed |CITS:[21040754]|. In addition, in 2007 Koch et al. showed that the expression of this gene was affected positively by iron; it is uncertain whether this gene is activated by FUR |CITS:[ 17120142]|.

name: rcnAB
note: In response to increasing concentrations of nickel or cobalt, RcnR releases the repression of rcnA and rcnR to allow its expression |CITS:[16956381][19703465]|. It has been recently determined that the expression levels of the rcnA and rcnR genes are directly induced by Ni and Co via the metallo-regulator RcnR, and both genes are differentially expressed |CITS:[21040754]|.

name: yohO
note: The mRNA produced by the yohO gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: yeiH
note: Based on microarray and RT-qPCR analyses, it was determined that the yeiH and azoR genes are upregulated upon exposure to glutathione (GSH) |CITS:[29468195]|.

name: pdeN
note: Base on a σS-deficient rpoS mutant background, rtn is under the significant negative control of rpoS |CITS:[19332833]|. This negative control is most likely due to increased formation of a vegetative or other alternative RNAP holoenzyme in the absence of competition of σS with the other sigma factors |CITS:[19332833]|. This suggests that σS might control rtn expression indirectly.

name: yejG
note: Based on DNA microarray analysis, the mechanism of bacterial inactivation by carvacrol and citral was studied |CITS: [28644990]|. Treatment by both compounds caused membrane damage and activated metabolism through the production of nucleotides required for DNA and RNA synthesis and metabolic processes |CITS: [28644990]|. A total of 76 and 156 genes demonstrated significant transcriptional differences by carvacrol and citral, respectively. Genes upregulated by carvacrol treatment included the multidrug efflux pump genes acrA and mdtM, genes related to the phage shock response, pspA, pspB, pspC, pspD, pspF, and pspG, and genes whose products are important for biosynthesis of arginine (argC, argG, artJ) and purine nucleotides (purC, purM). Genes upregulated by citral treatment included purH, pyrB, and pyrI. On the other hand, mutations in several differentially expressed genes confirmed the roles of ygaV, yjbO, pspC, sdhA, yejG, and ygaV in mechanisms of inactivation by carvacrol and citral |CITS: [28644990]|.
The G/C-rich discriminator sequence is located just downstream from the -10 box in the rplY promoter, suggesting possible negative regulation by ppGpp during the stringent response |CITS:[25749694]|.

name: atoSC
note: The transcription of the atoCS operon is activated by putrescine or diaminopropane polyamines |CITS:[17224065]|.

name: arnBCADTEF
note: arnBCAD transcript levels are increased under growth conditions with low Mg2+ concentrations |CITS: [25605366]|. arnA gene expression is downregulated upon L-valine addition (isoleucine starvation) in the cellular growth medium |CITS: [32211022]|; this effect was observed during the first 5 minutes of the amino acid addition, but then arnA expression returned to steady-state levels |CITS: [32211022]|.

name: elaB
note: The transcription of elaB is induced in the stationary phase when the cells are growing principally in minimal medium rather than LB medium. elaB expression contributes to cell survival under oxidative and heat shock stress conditions. On the other hand, the deletion of elaB increased the formation of persister cells |CITS:[ 28242719]|.

name: alaA
note: Alanine and leucine modestly repressed the avtA and alaA genes via Lrp |CITS:[20729367]|.

name: folC-dedD
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for folC . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: yfdC
note: The expression of the gene yfdC is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: yfdPQ
note: A transcript that contains yfdQ is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: oxc
note: Based on microarray and RT-qPCR analyses, it was determined that the asr, frc, oxc, ydeO, and yegR acid stress genes are significantly upregulated upon exposure to exogenous glutathione (GSH) |CITS:[29468195]|.

name: pdeA
note: Base on a σS-deficient rpoS mutant background, yfeA is under the significant negative control of rpoS |CITS:[19332833]|. This negative control is most likely due to increased formation of a vegetative or other alternative RNAP holoenzyme in the absence of competition of σS with the other sigma factors |CITS:[19332833]|. This suggests that σS might control yfeA expression indirectly.

name: ligA-ypeB
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ligA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: zipA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for zipA. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: murR
note: murR and murQ are divergently orientated and expressed from nonoverlapping face-to-face (convergent) promoters, and their transcripts are complementary at the 5' end. CRP controls positively murQP expression and it also may affect murR transcription as a consequence of this unusual promoter arrangement (face-to-face) |CITS:[18723630]|. CRP and MurR both repress murR expression beyond transcription initiation, possibly by generating a roadblock for RNA polymerase |CITS:[18723630]|. Autoinduction of murR is carried out by an activated form of MurR, which may be an activator form after interaction with an as yet unidentified effector, possibly a IIBC component, since most phosphorylated sugars can trigger activation of murR in a MurR-dependent manner |CITS:[19897902]|.

name: yffL
note: Based on ChIP-chip and consensus sequences (Virtual Footprint), a site of NsrR negatively controlling the yffL gene has been identified |CITS:[19656291]|. The consensus sequence found for this gene is ATAATGGAATAA, which lies in the intergenic region intZ-yffL.

name: eutSPQTDM
note: J. Oberto in 2010 identified a possible binding site for NagC, GTTATTTACTCTGACGAAAAATT, in the upstream region of the eutS gene. The demonstration was based on different statistical methods |CITS:[21070640]|.

name: glmY
note: The glmY gene is transcribed from two overlapping promoters: one of them is σ70 (glmYp2) and the other is σ54 dependent (glmYp1) |CITS:[19843219]|. Both promoters start transcription at the same nucleotide; however, they are active during different growth phases, as glmYp1 is mainly active during the transition to stationary phase and glmYp2 is active during exponential phase |CITS:[19843219]|. The overlapping of promoters upstream of glmY is a conserved feature in other enterobacterial species |CITS:[19843219]|. The activity of the glmYp1 promoter is dependent on the presence of the transcription factors GlrR and IHF. GlrR binds to three sites, designated ABS1, ABS2, and ABS3|CITS: [ 19843219]|.They are located at -221.5, -188.5, and -125.5 upstream of the transcription start site, respectively |CITS: [19843219 ]|. While ABS2 and ABS3 are essential for the activity of glmYp1, ABS1 only contributes slightly in the transcription of glmY |CITS: [19843219]|. IHF binds to two sites, located at -169 and -52 upstream of the transcription start site |CITS:[20965974]|. The glmY gene is located upstream of the gene cluster glrK-yfhG-glrR in the genome of all species of the Enterobacteriaceae family |CITS:[20965974]|.

name: kgtP
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for kgtP . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: raiAZ
note: Analysis of gene expression has proved that under NlpE lipoprotein overexpression, the presence of the protein CpxR causes increased transcription of the raiA gene; however, no DNA sequence related to the consensus DNA-binding site of CpxR has been identified in the regulatory region of the gene. Therefore, the effect of CpxR on raiA is probably indirect |CITS: [23564175]|. raiAp expression is increased during the transition from exponential- to stationary-phase growth |CITS: [2181444][11168583][23475967]| and decreased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: ypjB
note: Expression levels of cspI, ypjB, and yhjX genes were repressed by overproduction of YehT |CITS:[22685278]|. Insomuch as all of these genes lack any LytTR-like sequence motif, it is probable that the effect of YehT on these genes is indirect |CITS:[22685278]|.

name: ygaQ
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of ygaQ |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: kbp
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the ygaU gene is increased by CpxR |CITS: [23564175]|.

name: yqaE
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the yqaE gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: ygaVP
note: The ygaVP operon is activated by TBT (TriBuTyltin) and it is likely autoregulated by YgaV. In the absence of TBT, YgaV acts as a repressor, whereas the presence of TBT abolishes YgaV-mediated repression. TBT is not the natural inducer of the ygaVP operon, since it is a human-made compound |CITS: [18245262]|. Based on DNA microarray analysis, the mechanism of bacterial inactivation by carvacrol and citral was studied |CITS: [28644990]|. Treatment by both compounds caused membrane damage and activated metabolism through the production of nucleotides required for DNA and RNA synthesis and metabolic processes |CITS: [28644990]|. A total of 76 and 156 genes demonstrated significant transcriptional differences by carvacrol and citral, respectively. Genes upregulated by carvacrol treatment included the multidrug efflux pump genes acrA and mdtM, genes related to the phage shock response, pspA, pspB, pspC, pspD, pspF, and pspG, and genes whose products are important for biosynthesis of arginine (argC, argG, artJ) and purine nucleotides (purC, purM). Genes upregulated by citral treatment included purH, pyrB, and pyrI. On the other hand, mutations in several differentially expressed genes confirmed the roles of ygaV, yjbO, pspC, sdhA, yejG, and ygaV in mechanisms of inactivation by carvacrol and citral |CITS: [28644990]|.

name: gshA
note: gshA is preferentially transcribed by Eσ70 in vivo |CITS:[21398637]|.

name: yqaBA
note: By using ChIP-Seq analysis, it was observed that the protein PdeL binds upstream of the gene yqaB |CITS: [34903045]|.

name: casABCDE12
note: HNS binds in tandem at the casA-cas3 intergenic region, and it represses the transcription of the casA gene by inhibiting the RNA polymerase interaction with the casAp promoter. Analyses for footprinting show that HNS protects two long regions without defining the central positions of the binding sites of the protein HNS; for this reason, we only show the sequence of the hypersensitive site, TCGTTTTATT |CITS:[20126629]|. HNS also represses the expression of the anti-Pcas divergent promoter, which is reported to produce an RNA with a length between 150 and 200 bp. There exists the possibility that this divergent promoter affects the transcription or translation of the ygcB gene. H-NS and LeuO are antagonistic regulators of casABCDE-cas12 |CITS: [20659289]|. LeuO counteracts H-NS-dependent repression of casAp by reorganizing the DNA-protein contacts within the transcription initiation region |CITS: [20659289]|. Repression of the CRP in the casABCDE-cas1-cas2 operon is H-NS independent |CITS:[24720807]|. In fact, both compete for binding to the casAp promoter |CITS:[24720807]|. The casABCDE-cas12 operon is repressed by the StpA protein when H-NS is absent |CITS:[32353388]|.

name: cas3
note: Glycine, serine, N5,N10-methylene tetrahydrofolate (N5,N10-mTHF), cAMP, CRP, or the presence of the gcvTHP operon is able to induce the expression of the cas3 gene. The gcvTHP operon produces the enzyme to degrade glycine. A product of degradation of glycine or serine is N5,N10-mTHF, which appears to affect the production of cAMP, the effector of the transcriptional regulator CRP |CITS: [31941083]|.

name: queF
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of queF |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: ptrA-recBD
note: The transcript of ptrA is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: ygeA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ygeA. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: ygfB-pepP-ubiHI
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of pepP |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: argO
note: The activation by both Lrp and ArgP in the argO operator is intertwined, each activator being more potent in the absence of the other |CITS:[19906180]|. The competitive activation of a single promoter by two activator proteins, as described by Peeters et al., appears to be rare |CITS:[19906180]|. The three sites of the Lrp activator act cooperatively and normally in wrapping of the operator DNA in the argO promoter region |CITS:[19906180]|. Expression of argO is modulated by the effectors of both activators, which ensures an adapted and fine-tuned synthesis of the transporter in response to environmental conditions |CITS:[19906180]|. In 1999 Laishram et al. proposed that ArgP binds to a region of approximately 60 bp in the intergenic region of argO |CITS: [17504942][19906180]|. The curator assigned two binding sites of 20 bp for ArgP, located at -33.5 and -53.5, and proposes that in this intergenic region this regulator binds as dimer of dimers to two contiguous degenerated inverted repeat sites. The curator identified the consensus sequence of ArgP and assigned the possible central position of these binding sites, based on similarity to consensus sequences. ArgP has been identified as binding as a dimer of dimers in the intergenic region of dnaA in E. coli |CITS: [9254708]| and also in the regulatory region of gdhA of Klebsiella pneumoniae |CITS: [18424527]|. For this reason, there is a possibility that this regulator transcriptionally regulates as a dimer of dimers.

name: tktA
note: The expression of the gene tktA is enhanced under oxygen limitation |CITS: [30810277]|.

name: rsmE-gshB
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rsmE. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: yghED
note: The expression of an antisense RNA that starts downstream of the yghE gene is inhibited by RNA polymerase-binding RNA aptamers (RAPs) and the protein Rho |CITS: [31535128]|.

name: yghJ
note: By using ChIP-Seq analysis, it was observed that the protein PdeL binds upstream of the gene yghJ |CITS: [34903045]|.

name: yqhD-dkgA
note: A transcript containing the intergenic region of the yqhD and dkgA genes was identified by RT-PCR, suggesting that these two genes are part of the same operon|CITS: [20676725]|.

name: ygiQ
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ygiQ. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: mqsRA
note: Transcription of mqsR was found to be induced upon biofilm formation |CITS: [14727089]|, and deletion of mqsR decreases biofilm formation and abolishes motility |CITS: [16352847]|. mqsR is more highly expressed in persister cells than nonpersisters, and overexpression of mqsR leads to growth arrest and increased tolerance to certain antibiotics |CITS: [16768798][18309357]|. In addition, the toxins Hha, HokA, and CspD, the outer membrane proteins OppA and YbfM, and small RNA regulator Hfq are involved during persister cell formation via MqsR overexpression |CITS:[19909729]|. Coinduction of mqsA expression rescues cell growth |CITS: [19690171][19943910]|. However, the opposite effect has also been reported |CITS: [18309357]|.

name: mdaB
note: mdaB gene expression is positively controlled by MarA and SoxS |CITS:[21097628]|.

name: tolC-ygiBC
note: The expression of the tolC gene, and probably the expression of the complete tolC-ygiABC operon, is affected by tetracycline antibiotic pressure. An increase in tolC expression is maintained during exposition to increasing concentrations of tetracycline |CITS:[17426813]|. One marbox can activate transcription of tolCp3 and tolCp4 expression through the MarA, SoxS and Rob transcriptional regulators. This marbox is unique among the known marA/soxS/rob regulon promoters |CITS:[18673442]|. There is a consensus sequence for EnvgA in the upstream region of the tolC gene. In fact, increases in transcription of tolC from overproduction of EvgA have been mentioned by Masuda and Church (2002) and Nishino et al. (2003) |CITS:[12399493][12670992]|. However, Eguchi et al. (2003) showed reported that direct binding of EnvgA to the tolC promoter was not detected in their DNase I footprinting analysis (data not shown in the report) |CITS:[14523115]|. In tolC mutants, the transcription levels of the marA/soxS/rob regulon and tolC itself are elevated. Based on these findingsis, Rosner and Martin (2009) inferred that the following homeostatic loop could be present in wild-type bacteria: (i) normal metabolism results in the generation of certain intracellular metabolites that trigger the upregulation of the transcriptional activators MarA, SoxS, and Rob; (ii) these transcriptional activators, in turn, upregulate tolC, increasing the capacity for excretion of the metabolites via TolC; and (iii) the resulting reduction in the concentrations of the trigger metabolites (TMs) restores the basal levels of the activators. In tolC, mutants, the metabolites are not as effectively excreted, and the activator levels remain elevated |CITS:[19502391]|. In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid did not affect the expression of tolC, whereas it did affect other genes |CITS: [31271063]|. Review about the regulation of the tolC gene |CITS: [29128373]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ygiC. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|. Gene expression of tolC appears to be induced by 1,4-dihydroxy-2-naphthoic acid (DHNA) in a MarR- and SoxS-dependent way |CITS: [35648476]|.

name: sroG-ribB
note: SroG is generated from the 5' UTR of the ribB gene. It is expressed during exponential phase with a half life of 3 minutes |CITS: [14602901]|.

name: sibD
note: Based on Northern and 5'-3' RACE analysis, the sibD gene produces two distinct transcripts. The 3' end shorter transcript could be a result of processing |CITS:[18710431]|.

name: ygiF-glnE
