note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the glnE gene was increased in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, the gene expression was decreased. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on the gene expression |CITS:[21272324]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ygiF and glnE . This structure could regulate the expression of the genes, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: fadH
note: Repression of fadH transcription by the ArcAB system is additive to FadR repression under anaerobic conditions |CITS:[20622065]|.

name: cyuPA
note: yhaM is part of the yhaOM operon involved in the detoxification of cysteine. The absence of yhaM increases the inhibitory effect on bacterial growth caused by cysteine |CITS:[27435271]|. The cutPA operon showed the greatest differential expression in cutR transcript profiling |CITS: [28607157]|. In addition, under anaerobic conditions cutP required expression of cysteine, insomuch as it was induced by L-cysteine, D-cysteine, and a few sulfur-containing compounds during stationary phase |CITS: [28607157]|.

name: yhbW
note: yhbW gene expression is positively controlled by MarA and SoxS |CITS:[21097628]|.

name: deaD
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for deaD . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: nlpI
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for nlpI . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: degQS
note: The expression of the degS gene, and probably of the complete degQS operon (which codes for proteases), is affected by antibiotic pressure. As the tetracycline antibiotic concentration increases, the gene expression also increases. The role of the induced protease could be the degradation of porins to protect the cell against the antibiotic |CITS:[17426813]|.

name: yhcN
note: The transcript of yhcN is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: yhdP
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for yhdP. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: yhdV
note: The transcription of the yhdV gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: arfA
note: The hairpin structure shown here as a "terminator" is in fact not a transcriptional terminator, but the site of RNase III cleavage of the mRNA |CITS: [21435036]|.

name: bfd-bfr
note: Hepcidin, an antimicrobial peptide, increases the expression of bfd under conditions of iron availability, in a Fur-independent way |CITS:[29659799]|. The transcription of the bfd gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: yhfZY
note: By making use of microarray and ChIP assays and by σ54-related promoter element identification, it was proposed that yhfZ transcription is started from a σ54 promoter |CITS:[19969540]|; however, the sequence proposed as a promoter is inside the gene. Therefore, we did not assign this promoter to the gene.

name: rlmJ
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rlmJ . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: mdtEF
note: mdtE expression is increased at least 5-fold by indole |CITS: [15686558]|. ZraR activates mdtE expression |CITS:[30389436]|. The mdtEF-tolC, malEFG, gadBC, treBC, entCEBA-ybdB, hdeAB-yhiD, and alaE operons were differentially regulated after induction of the nonnative organic acid citramalate |CITS:[31186337]|.

name: yhjA
note: FNR and OxyR, which occupy the yhjAp promoter simultaneously, respond to two seemingly incompatible signals: oxygen starvation and peroxide stress |CITS:[17464064]|.

name: yhjD
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for yhjD. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: rdlD
note: Antisense-RNA rdlD controls ldrD gene expression, functioning as a trans-acting regulator at a posttranscriptional level |CITS:[12123448]|.

name: eptB
note: MgrR, a small RNA (sRNA), strongly negatively regulates eptB |CITS:[23659637]|. PhoQP also negatively regulates eptB expression, indirectly via the mgrR sRNA |CITS:[23659637]|.

name: avtA
note: Alanine and leucine modestly repressed the avtA and alaA genes via Lrp |CITS:[20729367]|.

name: yibF
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for yibF . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: gpmM-envC-yibQ
note: The gpmM gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: xanP
note: By using ChIP-Seq analysis, it was observed that the protein PdeL binds in the intergenic region of xanP and gltS |CITS: [34903045]|.

name: emrD
note: During an experiment using increasing concentrations of tetracycline, the transcription of the emrD gene was induced in low concentrations (1.5 and 4 mg/L), but this induction decreased in high concentrations (10 mg/L) |CITS:[17426813]|. It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of emrD |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: dgoRKADT
note: Based on a combination of genetic, functional, and biochemical analyses, regulatory elements were identified in the regulatory region of the dgoRKADT operon |CITS:[29728462]|.

name: mioC
note: The mioC promoter is stringently regulated |CITS:[2427203]|. The sequence close to its transcription start point is GC-rich, and there is a poor fit to the consensus sequence in the -35 region of the gid promoter; these two conserved features are shared by promoters under stringent control |CITS:[2581696]|.

name: ravA-viaA
note: The ravA-viaA operon is induced in stationary phase by σS under aerobic growth conditions |CITS: [16301313]|. σS differentially modulates the expression of the ravA and via genes under oxygen-limiting conditions |CITS: [27979649]|. A 7-nt consensus sequence for the NarL transcription factor (TACTCCT), centered 95 nt upstream of the ravA-viaA operon, was identified. However, mutation of this site had an effect on β-galactosidase activity |CITS: [27979649]|.

name: yieP-hsrA
note: The deletion of the Fis DNA binding sites located upstream of the yiePp gene enhances the inhibition of this gene via transcription-coupled DNA supercoiling (TCDS) in the early log phase. Fis appears to block supercoiling diffusion close to yiePp |CITS: [31639222]|. The transcription of the yieP gene is repressed during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: rhlB
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of rhlB |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: rmuC
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of rmuC |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: yihPO
note: The transcription of the yihO gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: yihUTS
note: The yihT gene is activated only during growth on lactose |CITS:[29453395]|. YihW is a dual regulator during growth in glucose and lactose, and its functions is sugar dependent |CITS:[29453395]|. yihT is repressed by CRP during growth on both lactose and glucose |CITS:[29453395]|. Based on RNA-seq, primer extension, and computational prediction, three promoters (yihTp1, yihTP2p2, and yihTp3), +93/+94, +35, and +45 relative to the ATG codon, respectively, were identified downstream of the yihT ATG codon, which suggests incorrect identification of either the translational start or their regulatory role in yihT transcription |CITS:[29453395]|.

name: yihV-csqR
note: Expression of yihV is increased during growth on lactose |CITS:[29453395]|. Both YihW and CRP function as repressors for yihV during growth in glucose |CITS:[29453395]|. CsqR represses the expression of the yihV gene during the exponential growth phase |CITS:[30372406]|. In the presence of the effectors sulfoquinovose (SQ) and sulfoquinovosyl glycerol (SQG), CsqR is inactivated, which derepresses the expression of the yihV gene |CITS:[30372406]|.

name: fdhD
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for fdhD . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: eptC
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the eptC gene is decreased |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: nudC-hemE-nfi
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for hemE . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: xylE
note: Based on a combination of genetic, functional, and biochemical analyses, regulatory elements were identified in the regulatory region of xylE |CITS:[29728462]|.

name: dgkA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for dgkA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: yjbJ
note: The expression of the gene yjbJ is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: adiC
note: The expression of the gene adiC is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression under aerobiosis appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: yjeJ
note: The transcription of the yjeJ gene is repressed during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: epmB
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of epmB |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: yjfN
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the yjfN gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: bsmA
note: bsmA is preferentially transcribed by Eσ70 in vivo |CITS:[21398637]|.

name: cycA
note: The gene cycA is regulated positively by RpoS, but it is not known if the transcription start site of this TU or another is induced when transcription is initiated by an RpoS promoter |CITS: [35336222]|. The expression of cycA is induced in the presence of the amino acids leucine, isoleucine, alanine, norvaline, alpha-aminobutyrate, and serine, and it is repressed in the presence of glycine and alpha-ketobutyrate |CITS: [35336222]|. Lrp represses the gene cycA in the absence of leucine |CITS: [35336222]|.

name: tamAB-ytfP
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for tamB. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: ytfQRT-yjfF
note: A site for GalR (TGAAAGCGATTACA) was identified at -137.5 upstream of the translation start site of ytfQ |CITS: [24272778]|. However, it had no effect under the experimental conditions tested |CITS: [24272778]|.

name: ridA
note: ridA is indirectly regulated by BglG through the transcriptional regulator Lrp in stationary phase and under conditions where leucine antagonizes the effect of Lrp |CITS:[31063124]|.

name: bdcA
note: Based on transcription analysis with qRT-PCR and mutants, it was shown that BdcR (formerly YjgJ) is a negative regulator of the bdcA gene |CITS:[21059164]|.

name: lptFG
note: The transcription of the lptF gene appears to be positively regulated by the protein BglG |CITS: [35397010]|.

name: yjhIHG
note: The yjhIHG operon is induced by D-xylonate |CITS:[31482281]|. Based on computational analysis, another promoter was identified at absolute position 4525952. However, mutation analysis showed that this promoter plays a nonessential supporting role in yjhIp promoter activity |CITS:[31482281]|.

name: hypT
note: Transcription of qseD increases during stationary phase |CITS:[20494990]|.

name: mdtM-yjiN
note: Based on DNA microarray analysis, the mechanism of bacterial inactivation by carvacrol and citral was studied |CITS: [28644990]|. Treatment by both compounds caused membrane damage and activated metabolism through the production of nucleotides required for DNA and RNA synthesis and metabolic processes |CITS: [28644990]|. A total of 76 and 156 genes demonstrated significant transcriptional differences by carvacrol and citral, respectively. Genes upregulated by carvacrol treatment included the multidrug efflux pump genes acrA and mdtM, genes related to the phage shock response, pspA, pspB, pspC, pspD, pspF, and pspG, and genes whose products are important for biosynthesis of arginine (argC, argG, artJ) and purine nucleotides (purC, purM). Genes upregulated by citral treatment included purH, pyrB, and pyrI. On the other hand, mutations in several differentially expressed genes confirmed the roles of ygaV, yjbO, pspC, sdhA, yejG, and ygaV in mechanisms of inactivation by carvacrol and citral |CITS: [28644990]|.

name: lgoR
note: In the presence of D-glucuronate, the binding affinity of ExuR is higher than that of UxuR to the lgoR/lgoD intergenic region. Under the same conditions, the binding of ExuR to the regulatory region is affected by the presence of UxuR |CITS:[ 27129867]|.

name: holD-rimI-yjjG
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of yjjG |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: slt
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the slt gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: yggG
note: The expression of the gene loiP is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: setB
note: By using ChIP-Seq analysis, it was observed that the protein PdeL binds in the intergenic region of fruB and setB |CITS: [34903045]|.

name: yncJ
note: Analysis of gene expression has proved that under NlpE lipoprotein overexpression, the presence of the protein CpxR causes increased transcription of the yncJ gene; however, no DNA sequence related to the consensus DNA-binding site of CpxR has been identified in the regulatory region of the gene. Therefore, the effect of CpxR on yncJ is probably indirect |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: aroH
note: The expression of the gene aroH is enhanced under oxygen limitation |CITS: [30810277]|.

name: rpmI-rplT
note: rpmI may be regulated by CRP, based on a site identified via Selex analysis |CITS: [23475967]|.

name: thrS-infC-rpmI-rplT-pheMST-ihfA
note: The transcription pattern in the thrS-infC-rpmI-rplT-pheMST-infA cluster is highly complex and the mRNA level for each gene is determined by a variety of factors, including multiple promoters, cotranscription, and readthrough of transcription termination signals. Although there is no experimental evidence of a transcript that comprises the whole cluster of genes |CITS: [2838394]|, we would not like |CITS: [6317865]| to discard the possibility that these genes form an operon. In addition, there are at least six regions upstream thrS that could serve as promoters, based on homology to the Escherichia coli σ70 consensus sequence |CITS: [2838394]|.

name: norR
note: In assay experiments, it was demonstrated that disruption of any one of the three sites of the NorR transcriptional regulator prevented autogenous repression of norR transcription. All three sites are required not only for activation of the norVW operon but also for repression of the norR gene, since NorR acts cooperatively |CITS: [16417519]|.

name: asnC-mioC
note: About 50 % of the transcription initiated at the asnCp promoter reaches the end of the mioC gene |CITS: [2464126]|. In contrast to the effect of asparagine on the regulation of asnA by AsnC, this amino acid does not affect the autoregulation of AsnC|CITS: [2864330]|.

name: asnC-mioC-mnmG
note: About 20 % of the transcription initiated at the asnCp promoter reaches the end of the gidA gene. In contrast to the effect of asparagine on the regulation of asnA by AsnC, this amino acid does not affect the autoregulation of AsnC|CITS: [2864330]|. It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds in the intergenic region of the genes mnmG and mioC, which corresponds to the oriC region |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: smg
note: The transcription of the smg gene may be dependent on a promoter located within the smf gene, although the precise position was not located |CITS[15716138]|. The transcription of the smg gene is independent from the promoter upstream, smfp |CITS [15716138]|.

name: fliDST
note: In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of fliDS as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: yqjA-mzrA
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the mzrA gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: yjbEFGH
note: The yjbEFGH operon was previously shown to be involved in exopolysaccharide synthesis |CITS: [17379715]|, biofilm formation, and the Rcs phosphorelay system |CITS: [14651646]|. It is induced by osmotic stress |CITS: [12024246]| and is under positive control of the RpoS-regulated general stress response of E. coli |CITS: [15184550], [15716429]|. Nevertheless, it has been shown that RpoS has a negative and indirect effect on it |CITS: [17957352]|. Induction of yjbEFGH is restricted to relatively low cell densities and is positively affected by slow growth rates and a high oxygen concentration |CITS: [17957352]|. The expression of the operon yjbEFGH is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: glgBXCAP
note: The transcription of this transcription unit is induced during the exponential growth phase, reaching a plateau in the stationary phase |CITS:[21029047]|. Montero et al. |CITS:[21029047]| did not find experimentally the transcription start sites upstream of glgC that had been reported by T. Romeo and J. Preiss in 1989 |CITS:[ 2468650]|. Montero et al. suggested that only the promoters glgBp and glgAp transcribe the genes of this operon and that glgBp is the main promoter directing the expression of the operon |CITS:[21029047]|.

name: rlmM
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rlmM . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: rnlAB
note: That the rnlA and rnlB genes are located together in the genome, such that the terminal region of the first gene overlaps the start region of the second one, that there is not a terminator sequence of transcription at the end of rnlA, and that both genes encode a toxin-antitoxin system, suggest that they are transcribed as an operon |CITS:[ 20980243]|.

name: uppS-cdsA
note: The transcription unit uppS-cdsA is sustainably induced at low temperature |CITS: [31127859]|.

name: nemA
note: Although nemA and gloA are involved in the same metabolic pathway (degradation of toxic compounds for detoxification and reuse as nitrogen sources), it is not yet known whether these two genes are organized into a single operon |CITS:[18567656]|.

name: rnd
note: The rnd upstream region shows some unusual features, which include a stable hairpin structure between the transcription and translation start sites. This structure could be a ρ-independent transcription terminator, and its location only 18 nucleotides after the transcription start site of rnd mRNA is particularly puzzling |CITS:[2681190]|. In addition, the rnd upstream region utilizes an infrequently used UUG initiator codon |CITS:[2681190]|. Based on the primer extension analysis, the hairpin structure does not represent an rnd terminator or an RNA processing site |CITS:[2681190]|.

name: dfp
note: The dfp gene that is upstream of the dut-slmA operon, very close and in the same direction, has been proved to be transcribed separately from this operon, based on its independent mutant phenotype |CITS: [2999089]|. It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of dfp |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: ybgTE
note: This transcription unit is a prediction and includes two genes, ybgT-ybgE, that overlap and between them do not have an intergenic region. In addition, the promoter predictions in RegulonDB show that both genes have the same promoter prediction |CITS: [14529615]|.

name: ybiB
note: In conjunction with gene expression analysis, it can be argued that the ybiB and dinG genes form an operon regulated by LexA |CITS:[20718955]|. Based on crystallization analysis and EMSA, it was determined that YbiB binds to DNA with high affinity. The ybiB gene is under LexA control and is induced by DNA-damaging agents |CITS: [26063803] |.

name: mreBCD
note: Based on sequence analysis, |CITS: [1937035]| suggested that this transcription unit was shaped by five genes, mreBCD-yhdE-rng. However, |CITS:[17090951]| demonstrated through Northern blot and gene expression analyses that this operon has three genes only, mreBCD. This operon appears to be positively regulated by FlhDC |CITS:[11287152]|, but it is not known which of the three promoters transcribing the operon is affected by the protein.

name: mreBCD
note: Based on sequence analysis, |CITS: [1937035]| suggested that this transcription unit was shaped by five genes, mreBCD-yhdE-rng. However, |CITS:[17090951]| demonstrated through Northern blot and gene expression analyses that this operon has three genes only, mreBCD. This operon appears to be positively regulated by FlhDC |CITS:[11287152]|, but it is not known which of the three promoters transcribing the operon is affected by the protein.

name: mreB
note: The mreB gene appears to be positively regulated by FlhDC |CITS:[11287152]|, but it is not known which of the three promoters transcribing the gene is affected by the protein. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for mreB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: mreB
note: The mreB gene appears to be positively regulated by FlhDC |CITS:[11287152]|, but it is not known which of the three promoters transcribing the gene is affected by the protein. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for mreB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: mreB
note: The mreB gene appears to be positively regulated by FlhDC |CITS:[11287152]|, but it is not known which of the three promoters transcribing the gene is affected by the protein. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for mreB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: crp
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the crp gene was decreased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, the gene expression was increased in the Fur, cyoA, and cydB mutants. |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene crp is decreased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The decreased expression appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB, and also could be caused by the increased glucose concentration observed under acidic conditions |CITS:[23274360]|. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on the gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the three promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators and by acidic and nitrogen-rich growth conditions.

name: crp
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the crp gene was decreased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on the gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the three promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators and by acidic and nitrogen-rich growth conditions.

name: hisJQMP
note: ArgR regulator can control hisJ gene expression through topological changes on the promoter region, inhibiting RNA polymerase binding |CITS:[17850814]|.

name: emrE
note: During an experiment using increasing concentrations of tetracycline, the transcription of the emrE gene was induced in low concentrations (1.5 and 4 mg/L), but this induction decreased in high concentrations (10 mg/L) |CITS:[17426813]|.

name: fliLMNOPQR
note: In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of fliLMOPQR as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: ryjB
note: PhoP activates the transcription of ryjB under acid stress |CITS: [34967409]|. On the other hand, it was demonstrated that ryjB is not a target of σ38 |CITS: [34967409]|.

name: csgDEFG
note: High concentrations of intracellular GlcNAc-6P signal the cells to downregulate the csgBA and csgDEFG operons |CITS:[16816193]|.

name: ftsLIO-murEF-mraY-murD-ftsW-murGC-ddlB-ftsQAZ-lpxC
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ddlB, ftsZ and murF . This structure could regulate the expression of the genes, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: ftsLIO-murEF-mraY-murD-ftsW-murGC-ddlB-ftsQAZ-lpxC
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ddlB, ftsZ and murF . This structure could regulate the expression of the genes, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: wzbc
note: The expression of this operon  is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: yiaKLMNO-lyx-sgbHUE
note: Under aerobic conditions, expression of the yiaKMNO operon, together with the ula regulon, is necessary for l-ascorbate assimilation. This compound induces transcription of the operon at the end of the exponential phase and at the beginning of the stationary phase.

name: potFGHI
note: There is a putative weak transcriptional termination signal in the potG gene, so synthesis of a part of mRNA may stop at this point.

name: rpoD
note: The rpsU-dnaG-rpoD operon has several promoters which are activated under different conditions. This operon contains two terminators, an internal terminator (T1) at the intergenic region between the rpsU and dnaG genes and a rho-independent terminator at the end of the operon (T2). The internal terminator might respond to antitermination by the lambda phage N gene product. The expression of the gene rpoD is increased under acidic growth conditions |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: nagC
note: Two promoters localized within the nagA gene sequence are responsible for most of the nagC expression. Although mutational and S1 mapping analyses have been carried out by |CITS:[8626331]|, they did not point out the transcriptional start of nagCp1 and nagCp2, and they only mentioned the putative boxes for σ70-type promoters. Therefore, a putative transcriptional start site to these promoters was assigned based on the observation that the majority of the promoters with experimentally determined transcriptional start sites present a distance of 6 nucleotides between the transcriptional start site and the -10 box |CITS:[3550697]|.

name: nagC
note: Two promoters localized within the nagA gene sequence are responsible for most of the nagC expression. Although mutational and S1 mapping analyses have been carried out by |CITS:[8626331]|, they did not point out the transcriptional start of nagCp1 and nagCp2, and they only mentioned the putative boxes for σ70-type promoters. Therefore, a putative transcriptional start site to these promoters was assigned based on the observation that the majority of the promoters with experimentally determined transcriptional start sites present a distance of 6 nucleotides between the transcriptional start site and the -10 box |CITS:[3550697]|.

name: phoPQ
note: The expression of the gene phoP is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: creABC
note: The function of the creC gene could only be detected in the phoR mutants. The expression of the gene phoM (creC) is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|. This gene is the third in the operon in which it is transcribed, but an effect of these growth conditions and PhoB on the other genes in the operon has not been observed.

name: rrsA-ileT-alaT-rrlA-rrfA
note: E. coli has seven copies of the operon that encode ribosomal RNAs (rrn operons). This multiplicity of operons is necessary so as not to saturate individual rrn operons with RNApol, which would result in transcription-replication conflicts, a longer lag phase before growth resumes, cell death, DNA damage, mutations, and SOS induction |CITS: [35552441]|. The seven ribosomal operons are complexly transcribed. Each operon has two σ70 promoters (p1 and p2); their transcription starts with a large precursor that contains rRNAs |CITS: [15686546]|; they are the target of regulation by the alarmone ppGpp, several triphosphate nucleotides (NTPs), and the transcriptional regulators FIS and HNS. FIS and HNS regulate at the p1; however, ppGpp and NTPs control both promoters in accordance with their concentration changes, even in the absence of the transcriptional regulators |CITS: [15568992] [14526030] [12504278]|. In addition, these two small regulating metabolites (ppGpp and NTPs) require the help of the DskA coregulatory protein that binds to the RNA polymerase |CITS: [15568992]|. The net amount of rRNA transcription can be seen as a complex function of the ratio of inhibitors to activators. HNS/Fis and ppGpp/NTP ratios are the highest ones during slow growth and the lowest ones during rapid growth; the ribosomal production is in accordance to the growth rate (higher in rapid growth and lower in slow growth) |CITS: [15686546] [14526030]|. It is important to note that the ribosomal gene-containing operons also encode genes whose products interact with the RNA polymerase and participate in the translation elongation and in the replication apparatus. Reviews: |CITS: [15590778] [8531889] [7517053] [2434021] [15686546] [15568992]|. Additional reference:Keener J. and Nomura M. (1996). Regulation of Ribosome Synthesis, in: Neidhardt, F. (Editor in Chief) et al., E. coli and Salmonella: Cellular and Molecular Biology. ASM Press, Washington, D.C., p.1417-1431.

name: rrsE-gltV-rrlE-rrfE
note: E. coli has seven copies of the operon that encode ribosomal RNAs (rrn operons). This multiplicity of operons is necessary so as not to saturate individual rrn operons with RNApol, which would result in transcription-replication conflicts, a longer lag phase before growth resumes, cell death, DNA damage, mutations, and SOS induction |CITS: [35552441]|. The seven ribosomal operons are complexly transcribed. Each operon has two σ70 promoters (p1 and p2); their transcription starts with a large precursor that contains rRNAs |CITS: [15686546]|; they are the target of regulation by the alarmone ppGpp, several triphosphate nucleotides (NTPs), and the transcriptional regulators FIS and HNS. FIS and HNS regulate at the p1; however, ppGpp and NTPs control both promoters in accordance with their concentration changes, even in the absence of the transcriptional regulators |CITS: [15568992] [14526030] [12504278]|. In addition, these two small regulating metabolites (ppGpp and NTPs) require the help of the DskA coregulatory protein that binds to the RNA polymerase |CITS: [15568992]|. The net amount of rRNA transcription can be seen as a complex function of the ratio of inhibitors to activators. HNS/Fis and ppGpp/NTP ratios are the highest ones during slow growth and the lowest ones during rapid growth, and the ribosomal production is in accordance with the growth rate (higher in rapid growth and lower in slow growth) |CITS: [15686546] [14526030]|. It is important to note that the ribosome gene-containing operons also encode genes whose products interact with the RNA polymerase and participate in the translation elongation and in the replication apparatus. Reviews: |CITS: [15590778] [8531889] [7517053] [2434021] [15686546] [15568992]|. Additional reference:Keener J. and Nomura M. (1996). Regulation of Ribosome Synthesis, in: Neidhardt, F. (Editor in Chief) et al., E. coli and Salmonella: Cellular and Molecular Biology. ASM Press, Washington, D.C., p. 1417-1431.

name: clpPX
note: There is a possible factor-dependent transcription terminator downstream of clpP. clpX can be expressed independently from clpP by using its own promoter, but this promoter has not been clearly identified. The expression of the clpP gene, and probably the expression of the complete clpPX operon that codes for a protease, is affected by antibiotic pressure. As the antibiotic (tetracycline) concentration increases, the gene expression also increases. The role of the induced protease could be the degradation of porins to protect the cell against the antibiotic |CITS: [17426813]|.

name: pspE
note: The expression of the pspE gene is induced in a mutant at the YcjW transcriptional regulator; however, this appears to be an indirect effect of the regulator on gene expression |CITS: [31253770]|.

name: cadA
note: During acid stress, RavA antagonizes the inhibitory effect of ppGpp on CadA activity |CITS: [21148420]|. The mRNA produced by the cadA gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|. Fluoroquinolones and -lactams increased cadA expression, while an aminoglycoside (amikacin) decreased such expression |CITS: [34543694]|.

name: rrlH-rrfH
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rrlH. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: caiCDE
note: The transcript of caiE is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: rrlA-rrfA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rrlA. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: deoABD
note: It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of deoA |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: safA-ydeO
note: The transcription of safA appears to be increased under acidic growth conditions during the exponential phase in a RcsB-dependent manner, but not during stationary phase |CITS:[21571995]|. Based on cells with plasmids and three inducible promoters of genes (matA, safA, and chbB), three novel fluorescent biosensors sensitive to terahertz (THz) radiation were developed, and they controlled the expression of a fluorescent protein |CITS:[33014613]|. The induction of these biosensors was manifested as fluorescent luminosity and reflected the activity of transcription units matA, safA-ydeO, and chbBCARFG, which contain the transcription factors MatA, YdeO, and ChbR, respectively |CITS:[33014613]|.

name: casDE-ygbTF
note: ygcH, ygbT, and ygbF genes are transcribed together with three other genes located upstream of them. On the other hand, a σ32-dependent promoter was suggested to be located upstream of ygcI |CITS: [16892065]|. Therefore, we inferred that the four genes (ygcIH-ygbTF) form a transcription unit transcribed from the σ32 promoter.

name: yjjQ-bglJ
note: LeuO binds to three sites in the yjjQ-yjjP intergenic region (from -147 to -347 upstream of the yjjQp promoter). Although Stratmann et al. (2008) used a purified protein, they did not mention the exact position of LeuO in this region. The LeuO-binding site overlapping the yjjPp promoter is required for full derepression of the yjjQp promoter |CITS: [18055596]|. LeuO-binding-mediated DNA looping may prevent spreading of the H-NS complex into the promoter of the yjjQ-bglJ operon |CITS: [18055596]|.

name: secG-leuU
note: The only case in Escherichia coli of a gene encoding a protein and a tRNA is seen in the secG-leuU operon |CITS: [18033800]|.

name: secG-leuU
note: The only case in Escherichia coli of a gene encoding a protein and a tRNA is seen in the secG-leuU operon |CITS: [18033800]|.

name: cspE
note: The mRNA produced by the cspE gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: iraP
note: iraP gene expression is upregulated upon L-valine addition (isoleucine starvation) in the cellular growth medium |CITS: [32211022]|. J. Oberto in 2010 identified a possible binding site for NagC, AATAATTACCCACACAAAATATA, in the intergenic region of the divergent genes iraP and ddlA. This demonstration was based on different statistical methods |CITS:[21070640]|, but it is not known if NagC regulates transcription in both directions.

name: acrB
note: Inactivation or inhibition of AcrAB-TolC activates the acrAB operon in response to cellular metabolism, such as enterobactin, cystein, and purine biosynthesis, and gluconeogenesis |CITS:[24043404]|. A feedback regulatory model has been proposed where the absence of a functional AcrAB-TolC pump leads to the accumulation of cellular metabolites, which usually leave cells via an AcrAB-TolC multidrug efflux pump. This accumulation results in the inactivation of AcrR and the induction of soxS and marA expression, ultimately triggering upregulation of acrAB expression to restore homeostasis |CITS:[24043404]|. Based on the bacteriostatic antibiotic-induced persister model, 37 and 9 genes were found to produce defects in rifampin- and tetracycline-induced persister formation, respectively. Only six mutants were found to overlap in both treatment-induced persister screens: recA, recC, ruvA, uvrD, fis, and acrB. Four of these mutants (recA, recC, ruvA, and uvrD) mapped to the DNA repair pathway, one mutant mapped to a global transcriptional regulator (fis), and one to a gene for an efflux protein (acrB) |CITS:[29559967]|. Other genes that mapped to transporters, membrane biogenesis, LPS biosynthesis, flagellum biosynthesis, metabolism (folate and energy), and translation were more specific to rifampin-induced persisters |CITS:[29559967]|. Sodium salicylate (NaSal), acetyl salicylic acid (ASA), acetaminophen (APAP), and ibuprofen contribute to antibiotic resistance by inducing the expression of both marA and acrB genes |CITS:[30304479]|. The induction by NaSal, ASA, and APAP is relatively higher and is partly dependent on marA, whereas ibuprofen, which induces lower antibiotic resistance, shows complete marA dependence. NaSal, ASA, APAP, and ibuprofen induce antibiotic resistance in an acrB-dependent manner |CITS:[30304479]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for acrB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: clsC
note: The promoter activity for clsC was increased after cold shock exposure |CITS:[ 28582517]|. The clsC transcript is present during the exponential and stationary growth phases |CITS:[ 28582517]|. Overexpression of clsC enhanced biofilm formation and promoted apramycin resistance; this effect was dependent on the absence of the sucA gene. On the other hand, the biofilm formation and the resistance to apramycin were increased in single clsC knockout cells compared with wild-type cells. These effects were greater in a ymdA-ymdB-clsC triple knockout |CITS:[ 28582517]|.

name: dctA
note: Under NlpE outer membrane lipoprotein overexpression, the transcription of the dctA gene is decreased |CITS:[ 23564175]|.

name: acnA
note: The transcription of the gene acnA is enhanced under high oxygen saturation (300%) in the absence of the superoxide dismutase proteins SodA and SodB |CITS:[ 23497217]|. The acnA gene is downregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: trg
note: The trgp1 and aerp promoters are the only Escherichia coli σ28 promoters that are activated by CRP |CITS:[19843224]|.

name: tyrS-pdxY
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for tyrS. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: pykF
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the pykF gene increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, gene expression was decreased in the Fnr mutant. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the four promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: pykF
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the pykF gene increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, gene expression was decreased in the Fnr mutant. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the four promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: yeaGH
note: The yeaGH operon is highly expressed during the initial adaptive response to nitrogen starvation. It is also highly expressed in response to diverse stresses, including low pH |CITS:[ 15716429]|, hyperosmotic conditions |CITS:[ 11742689]|, entry into stationary phase |CITS:[ 15716429]|, nitrogen starvation |CITS:[24947454]|, sulfur limitation |CITS:[ 15659685]|, growth in biofilms |CITS:[12657059]|, and exposure to the antimicrobial peptide polymyxin B |CITS:[17010160]|. It is conserved across several bacterial species, especially among Enterobacteriaceae, including species pathogenic to plants or animals |CITS:[24947454][26621053][11121068]|, and alongside relA it constitutes a new branch in the Ntr response |CITS:[30514772]|.

name: pykA
note: The expression of the gene pykA is enhanced under oxygen limitation |CITS: [30810277]|.

name: gatZABCD
note: gatABC were found to be downregulated in a MG1655 lysogen carrying the Stx2a phage O104 |CITS: 31208335|.

name: rpoS
note: At the transcriptional level, (p)ppGpp positively affects rpoS transcript elongation and/or stability |CITS:[1849609]|. The expression of the gene rpoS is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression under aerobiosis appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|, but it is not known which promoter, of seven transcribing rpoS, is affected by PhoB.

name: rpoS
note: At the transcriptional level, (p)ppGpp positively affects rpoS transcript elongation and/or stability |CITS:[1849609]|. name: pyrG-eno

name: groL

note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for groL . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: groL
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for groL . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: dksA-yadB
note: Based on gene expression analysis, it was shown that dksA expression is controlled by YqhC |CITS:[20676725]|.

name: creD
note: The expression of the gene creD is increased when colicin (at 30 ng/ml) is added to the medium |CITS: [23421615]|.

name: nemRA
note: nemRA and gloA transcripts appear to be generated from the processing of the nemRA-gloA transcript by RNase E CITS:[23506073]|.

name: pstS
note: Repetitive extragenic palindrome (REP) displays a low transcription termination activity of the pstS gene. It also stabilizes to pstS mRNA and increases the level of PstS proteins |CITS:[18820899]|. The expression of the gene pstS is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: tolC-ygiBC
note: Method used to determine the Transcription Unit: RT-PCR analysis. In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid did not affect the expression of tolC, whereas it did affect other genes |CITS: [31271063]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ygiC. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: tolC-ygiBC

This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: tolC-ygiBC
note: One marbox can activate transcription of tolCp3 and tolCp4 expression through the MarA, SoxS and Rob transcriptional regulators. This marbox is unique among the known marA/soxS/rob regulon promoters |CITS:[18673442]|. There is a consensus sequence for EnvgA in the upstream region of the tolC gene. In fact, increases in transcription of tolC from overproduction of EvgA have been mentioned by Masuda and Church (2002) and Nishino et al. (2003) |CITS:[12399493][12670992]|. However, Eguchi et al. (2003) showed reported that direct binding of EnvgA to the tolC promoter was not detected in their DNase I footprinting analysis (data not shown in the report) |CITS:[14523115]|. In tolC mutants, the transcription levels of the marA/soxS/rob regulon and tolC itself are elevated. Based on these findingsis, Rosner and Martin (2009) inferred that the following homeostatic loop could be present in wild-type bacteria: (i) normal metabolism results in the generation of certain intracellular metabolites that trigger the upregulation of the transcriptional activators MarA, SoxS, and Rob; (ii) these transcriptional activators, in turn, upregulate tolC, increasing the capacity for excretion of the metabolites via TolC; and (iii) the resulting reduction in the concentrations of the trigger metabolites (TMs) restores the basal levels of the activators. In tolC, mutants, the metabolites are not as effectively excreted, and the activator levels remain elevated |CITS:[19502391]|. In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid did not affect the expression of tolC, whereas it did affect other genes |CITS: [31271063]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ygiC. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|. Gene expression of tolC appears to be induced by 1,4-dihydroxy-2-naphthoic acid (DHNA) in a MarR- and SoxS-dependent way |CITS: [35648476]|.

name: murQP
note: murR and murQ are divergently orientated and expressed from nonoverlapping face-to-face (convergent) promoters, and their transcripts are complementary at the 5' end. CRP controls positively murQP expression and it also may affect murR transcription as a consequence of this unusual promoter arrangement (face-to-face) |CITS:[18723630]|.

name: murQP
note: murR and murQ are divergently orientated and expressed from nonoverlapping face-to-face (convergent) promoters, and their transcripts are complementary at the 5' end. CRP controls positively murQP expression and it also may affect murR transcription as a consequence of this unusual promoter arrangement (face-to-face) |CITS:[18723630]|.

name: sibE
note: Based on Northern and 5'-3' RACE analysis, the sibE gene produces two distinct transcripts. The 3' end shorter transcript could be a result of processing |CITS:[18710431]|. The transcription of the sibE gene is induced during the first 24 h after the induction of nitrogen starvation |CITS: [32571968]|.

name: sibC
note: Based on Northern and 5'-3' RACE analysis, the sibC gene produces two distinct transcripts. The 3' end shorter transcript could be a result of processing |CITS:[18710431]|.

name: gadW
note: Genes involved in the gad (gadABCDEWX) system are common under many adverse conditions, as determined by microarray analyses and Fourier transform-infrared spectroscopy. Although they are known to be important for the acid stress response, it has also been shown that part of this system is also upregulated by NaCl, cold stress, ethanol, and heat stress |CITS:[19767843]|. The sequence of the putative stem-loop structure located 334 nt downstream of the gadW stop codon reportedly may function as a rho-independent terminator |CITS:[18808381]|; however, the putative terminator sequence was not shown in the paper. The expression of the gene gadW is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: gadW
note: Genes involved in the gad (gadABCDEWX) system are common under many adverse conditions, as determined by microarray analyses and Fourier transform-infrared spectroscopy. Although they are known to be important for the acid stress response, it has also been shown that part of this system is also upregulated by NaCl, cold stress, ethanol, and heat stress |CITS:[19767843]|. The expression of the gadW gene was induced by SidA in the presence of N-(3-oxo-hexanoyl)-L-homoserine lactone (AHL) at 30 C, although the expression was negatively responsive in the presence of ethyl acetate (EA) at 37 C |CITS:[20126629]|. It is not known if the regulation of the gadW gene by SdiA is dependent on the gadWp1 or gadWp2 promoter or both. The transcription of gadW appears to be increased under acidic growth conditions during the exponential phase in a RcsB-dependent manner, but not during stationary phase |CITS:[21571995][23274360]|. However, it is not known which of the three promoters that transcribe this gene is affected by RcsB. The sequence of the putative stem-loop structure located 334 nt downstream of the gadW stop codon reportedly may function as a rho-independent terminator |CITS:[18808381]|; however, the putative terminator sequence was not shown in the paper.

name: uvrB
note: The full activity of the uvrB promoter region requires three promoters (uvrBp1, uvrBp2, and uvrBp3) and is repressed by both the DnaA and LexA proteins |CITS:[29967594]|. In the absence of the SOS response, both DnaA and LexA repress transcription of the uvrB gene and function independently of each other, but in an additive manner |CITS:[29967594]|.

name: gadEF
note: Genes involved in the gad (gadABCDEWX) system are common under many adverse conditions, as determined by microarray analyses and Fourier transform-infrared spectroscopy. Although they are known to be important for the acid stress response, it has also been shown that part of this system is also upregulated by NaCl, cold stress, ethanol, and heat stress |CITS:[19767843]|. The expression of the gene gadE is increased under acidic growth conditions in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The expression of gadE appears to be slightly repressed by Rob under butanol stress |CITS: [33072717]|.

name: ogt
note: Derrick et al. (2009) suggested that induction of ogt by NarL alone in response to external nitrate provides a prophylactic effect against possible genotoxicity arising from nitrate metabolism |CITS:[19245365]|. NarL activates the ogt promoter independently of FNR |CITS:[19245365]|. NarL (E273 residue) appears to interact directly with RNA polymerase (α-CTD) to activate transcription of the ogt promoter |CITS: [32662815]|. The ogt promoter is induced by NarL in response to nitrate ions, without help from FNR |CITS:[19245365]|. Based on experiments utilizing a mutant background, it was shown that NarP plays little or no role in the ogt promoter region |CITS:[19245365]|.

name: gadE-mdtEF
note: The expression of gadE appears to be slightly repressed by Rob under butanol stress |CITS: [33072717]|.

name: rfaYZ
note: The Rho protein appears to negatively regulate expression of the operon waaYZ |CITS: [33329496]|. Rho loading sites (rut sites) were identified upstream of each gene of the operon |CITS: [33329496]|.

name: mdh
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the mdh gene increased in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, gene expression was decreased in the cyoA and cydB and Fnr mutants. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on the gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the two promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene mdh is decreased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid decreased the expression of the mdh gene |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: citCDEFXG
note: Transcription of the citCDEFXG operon is dependent on CitB when E. coli is grown in the presence of citrate and the absence of nitrate and oxygen |CITS: [22101843]|. Yamamoto et al. in 2008 showed that CitB (DpiA), in the presence of citrate, activates the transcription of three promoters, citCp2, citCp1, and citAp (dpiBp), in the intergenic region located between the citC and citA genes (dpiB) |CITS: [18997424]|. But they have not shown the mechanism of action of CitB in the activation of the divergent operons.

name: ahpCF
note: Expression of ahpC is activated by OxyR. This is also true for the expression of the divergently transcribed dsbG. The intergenic region between dsbG and ahpC carries two binding sites for OxyR, a dsbG-proximal site, located 54 bp upstream of the dsbG start codon, and an ahpC-proximal site, located 290 bp upstream of the dsbG start codon |CITS:[11443092]|. The OxyR-regulated ahpCF operon is upregulated by methylglyoxal (MG), based on ChIP-chip analysis with DNA-RNAP occupancy |CITS:[23646895]|. Northern blotting and primer extension assays showed that OxyR binding to the ahpC-proximal site leads to the induction of both dsbG and ahpC transcripts, while OxyR binding to the dsbG-proximal site leads to the induction of a second ahpC transcript. This transcript of ahpC and the transcript of dsbG overlap by over 100 nucleotides. |CITS:[11443092]|. |CITS:[ 12589799]| demonstrated that both the oxyR and ahpC genes, which codify a transcriptional regulator and a product essential for the detoxification against H2O2-induced stress, respectively, were absolutely dependent on polyamines during entry into the stationary phase. These data suggest that polyamines directly participates in the defense mechanism against oxidative stress. Six RNA processing sites (PSSs) have been identified in the ahpCF operon by high-throughput screening of PSSs |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: ycgZ-ymgA-ariR-ymgC
note: ycgZ-ymgA-ariR-ymgC constitutes an operon. YmgA and AriR activate production of the biofilm matrix substance colanic acid as well as acid resistance genes and the biofilm-associated bdm gene and they downregulate adhesive curli fimbriae |CITS:[17222132][17609140][17765265][19172264]|. YcgZ appears to counteract the activities of YmgA and YmgB |CITS:[19240136]|. Expression of this operon is under the control of σ38 (rpoS) RNA polymerase and repressed by ycgE, which is transcribed divergently on the opposite strand |CITS:[19240136][ 15716429]|.

name: iraD
note: iraD is involved in posttranslational regulation of RpoS |CITS: [18383615]|. iraD promotes accumulation of RpoS, which binds to the adaptor RssB protein that targets RpoS for degradation |CITS: [19124769]| for proteolysis by ClpXP in vitro |CITS: [18383615]|. These results established the importance of RpoS stabilization in proliferating bacterial cells in which replication has been directly blocked, confirming a role for the RpoS regulon in DNA damage repair or tolerance. iraD transcription is induced by DNA damage via a mechanism independent of the SOS response, and both iraD and the SOS regulatory pathway could be acting synergistically to ensure survival of cells faced with oxidative or DNA damaging stress during cellular growth |CITS: [19124769]|. The expression of iraD is growth phase and ppGpp dependent |CITS: [19820090]|. ppGpp modulates iraD expression not only during the transition to stationary phase but also very sensitively in the mid-exponential phase |CITS: [19820090]|. Both RelA and SpoT are required for maximal iraD expression during exponential phase |CITS: [19820090]|. The effects of ppGpp and DksA on iraD regulatory expression are opposing, because DksA inhibits IraD expression, although this effect could be direct or indirect |CITS: [19820090]|.

name: azuCR
note: azuC gene expression is induced in acid medium at both transcriptional and posttranscriptional levels |CITS:[ 19734316]|. The AzuR regulatory small RNA has a base-pairing region (1,987,920 to 1,987,890) that overlaps with the coding region of azuC, thus directly competing with the translation of azuC |CITS: [35239434]|.

name: ykgR
note: ykgR gene expression is induced upon heat shock |CITS:[ 19734316]|.

name: yobF-cspC
note: The mRNA that contains the cspC gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: rraB
note: The transcription level of rraB is not affected by the growth phase, and it is transcribed throughout the exponential and stationary phases |CITS:[19717586]|. On the other hand, rraB is involved in the cell envelope stress response, and it is modulated either by GlcN-6-P or by metabolites derived from GlcN-6-P |CITS:[19717586]|.

name: glmY
note: The glmY gene is transcribed from two overlapping promoters: one of them is σ70 (glmYp2) and the other is σ54 dependent (glmYp1) |CITS:[19843219]|. Both promoters start transcription at the same nucleotide; however, they are active during different growth phases, as glmYp1 is mainly active during the transition to stationary phase and glmYp2 is active during exponential phase |CITS:[19843219]|. The overlapping of promoters upstream of glmY is a conserved feature in other enterobacterial species |CITS:[19843219]|. The glmY gene is located upstream of the gene cluster glrK-yfhG-glrR in the genome of all species of the Enterobacteriaceae family |CITS:[20965974]|.

name: nikABCDER
htpG
note: The htpG gene is upregulated by short-term (30 min) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: flhDC
note: The transcription of the flhDC operon is repressed in acidic phospholipid-deficient cells, but the gadW, metE, and yeaB genes in multicopy suppress this repression through the reduction of overexpression of RpoS caused by the same mutation in the gene producing acidic phospholipid |CITS:[20185506]|. The mechanism for this is not known. Under motility-inducing environmental growth conditions, a motile element (IS5) is inserted into the upstream region of the flhDC operon. This event appears to enhance the expression of the operon, because motility and biofilm formation are increased |CITS:[ 21390082]|. IS5 insertion disrupts the H-NS binding site, thus preventing transcriptional repression of the operon |CITS:[ 21390082]| In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of flhDC as well as other falgellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|. The mRNA that contains the flhD gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: acrD
note: During an experiment using increasing concentrations of tetracycline, the transcription of the acrD gene was induced in low concentrations (1.5 and 4 mg/L), but this induction decreased in high concentrations (10 mg/L) |CITS:[17426813]|. acrD expression is increased at least 5-fold by indole |CITS: [15686558]|.

name: greA
note: Potrykus et al. (2009) identified four promoters for the greA gene; however, based on two of them (called here the P3 and P4 promoters) they lack a clear consensus, they show no activity in vitro, and finally, lacZ fusion yields barely measurable activities. The P3 and P4 bands which were identified by primer extension could be generated during posttranscriptional processes |CITS:[20008510]|. greA gene expression is subject to self-repression. The autoregulatory effect is indirect, or additional factors are necessary |CITS:[20008510]|.

name: fnrS
note: The RNA produced by the fnrS gene has been observed mainly at the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: rnlB
note: Otsuka et al. in 2010 reported that there is a terminator sequence of transcription just downstream of the rnlB gene |CITS:[ 20421606]|, but the sequence was not reported. Therefore, we have not added this terminator to the database.

name: pfkA
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the pfkA gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, it is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the two promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene pfkA is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression of this gene is in agreement with the increased specific glucose consumption rate observed under acidic conditions |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: pykF
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the pykF gene increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, gene expression was decreased in the Fnr mutant. It is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the four promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: gor
note: The transcription of the gene gor is enhanced under high oxygen saturation (300%) |CITS:[ 23497217]|.

name: fliCX
note: In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of fliC as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: flgK
note: flgKL expression is enhanced by AtoSC in the presence of acetoacetate |CITS: [22083893]|. In a study where it was shown that the L-tryptophan eradicated persister cells, it was observed that the exogenous amino acid downregulated the expression of flgK as well as other flagellar genes |CITS: [31271063]|.

name: aer
note: The trgp1 and aerp promoters are the only Escherichia coli σ28 promoters that are activated by CRP |CITS:[19843224]|. J. Oberto in 2010 identified a possible binding site for NagC, GTTAATTATCTTGCCCAAAAATC, in the intergenic region of the divergent genes aer and patA. This demonstration was based on different statistical methods |CITS:[21070640]|, but it is not known if NagC regulates transcription in both directions.

name: tar-tap-cheRBYZ
note: A transcript that contains cheY is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|. The mRNA produced by the tar-tap-cheRBYZ operon has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|. The cheYZ genes are downregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: yhjH
note: Base on a σS-deficient rpoS mutant background, yhjH is under the significant negative control of rpoS |CITS:[19332833]|. This negative control is most likely due to increased formation of a vegetative or other alternative RNAP holoenzyme in the absence of competition of σS with the other sigma factors |CITS:[19332833]|. This suggests that σS might control yhjH expression indirectly.

name: cspA
note: cspA gene expression is repressed by MetJ, which is a protein that appears to bind to a site upstream of this gene, but it is not known which of two promoters (cspAp1, cspAp2) affects MetJ |CITS: [16515535]|. The cspA gene was significantly upregulated in bacterial cells associated with lettuce roots in a microarray analysis |CITS: [22830299]|.

name: cspA
note: cspA gene expression is repressed by MetJ, which is a protein that appears to bind to a site upstream of this gene, but it is not known which of two promoters (cspAp1, cspAp2) affects MetJ |CITS: [16515535]|. The cspA gene was significantly upregulated in bacterial cells associated with lettuce roots in a microarray analysis |CITS: [22830299]|. The mRNA produced by the cspA gene has been observed mainly in the poles of the cell |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: nanATEK-yhcH
note: nanATEK-yhcH, among other genes involved in carbon source transport and metabolism, were downregulated in two MG1655 lysogens carrying closely related Stx2a phages O104 and PA8 |CITS: 31208335|.

name: dcuC
note: The induction of dcuC is affected by FNR and ArcA regulators, but FNR plays the major role.

name: sodB
note: The proteins Fur and HNS regulate the transcription of the sodB gene; although a Fur-binding site has been identified in the regulatory region of sodB, the regulation of the two regulators appears to be indirect |CITS:[11782507]|. Transcriptional fusion experiments demonstrated a positive effect of Fur and a negative effect of HNS on sodB transcription |CITS:[11782507], [2180912],[ 15659676]|, and gel shift assays have shown a weak interaction between Fur and the regulatory region of sodB |CITS:[11782507][ 2180912]|. However, an in vitro transcription analysis demonstrated that Fur has no direct effect on the transcription of sodB |CITS:[11782507]|. It should be noted that Fur regulates in a negative way RhyB, a small RNA that negatively regulates the expression of sodB |CITS:[ 11917098]|. On the other hand, the effect of HNS on the sodB promoter depends on the presence of Fur; in addition, no curved DNA region was identified to bind HNS around the promoter region of sodB |CITS:[11782507]|.

name: asr
note: The asr gene was significantly upregulated in bacterial cells associated with lettuce roots in a microarray analysis |CITS: [22830299]|. The expression of asr is also increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The asr gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|. Based on microarray and RT-qPCR analyses, it was determined that the asr, frc, oxc, ydeO, and yegR acid stress genes are significantly upregulated upon exposure to exogenous glutathione (GSH) |CITS:[29468195]|.

name: fxsA
note: The transcript of fxsA is decreased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: osmC
note: The osmC gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|. The osmC gene is upregulated at 1.25 M MgSO4 (Magnesium Sulfate); this upregulatioin may arise from increased production of RpoS and the transcription of osmC from osmCp2, or in a RpoS-independent way from osmCp1 during the exponential growth phase. Another possibility is the downregulation of H-NS and LrP or decreased binding affinities to both promoters |CITS:[32349403]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for osmC . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: osmC
note: The osmC gene is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|. The osmC gene is upregulated at 1.25 M MgSO4 (Magnesium Sulfate); this upregulatioin may arise from increased production of RpoS and the transcription of osmC from osmCp2, or in a RpoS-independent way from osmCp1 during the exponential growth phase. Another possibility is the downregulation of H-NS and LrP or decreased binding affinities to both promoters |CITS:[32349403]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for osmC . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: ychF
note: Overexpression of YchF results in H<sub>2</sub> O<sub>2</sub> hypersensitivity |CITS:[23139412]|.

name: pspF
note: An integrated transcriptomic study of the extracytoplasmic stress response, which detects and responds to alterations of the bacterial envelope, was performed by Bury-Moré et al. (2009). They investigated the interconnections and dynamics between Bae, Cpx, Psp, Rcs, and σE pathways and showed that these pathways can be activated simultaneously in response to exogenous or endogenous stimulation, while natural environmental stimuli provoke bacterial modifications that lead to multiple pathway responses |CITS:[19763168]|. Each of these responses might be specialized to ensure a specific aspect of envelope biogenesis and maintenance; on the other hand, CpxR could have a role as a modulator of the response by integrating other endogenous signals. Bury-Moré et al. concluded that all five pathways are needed to mount a full response to extracytoplasmic stress |CITS:[19763168]|. Based on DNA microarray analysis, the mechanism of bacterial inactivation by carvacrol and citral was studied |CITS: [28644990]|. Treatment by both compounds caused membrane damage and activated metabolism through the production of nucleotides required for DNA and RNA synthesis and metabolic processes |CITS: [28644990]|. A total of 76 and 156 genes demonstrated significant transcriptional differences by carvacrol and citral, respectively. Genes upregulated by carvacrol treatment included the multidrug efflux pump genes acrA and mdtM, genes related to the phage shock response, pspA, pspB, pspC, pspD, pspF, and pspG, and genes whose products are important for biosynthesis of arginine (argC, argG, artJ) and purine nucleotides (purC, purM). Genes upregulated by citral treatment included purH, pyrB, and pyrI. On the other hand, mutations in several differentially expressed genes confirmed the roles of ygaV, yjbO, pspC, sdhA, yejG, and ygaV in mechanisms of inactivation by carvacrol and citral |CITS: [28644990]|.

name: pspF
note:
An integrated transcriptomic study of the extracytoplasmic stress response, which detects and responds to alterations of the bacterial envelope, was performed by Bury-Moré et al. (2009). They investigated the interconnections and dynamics between Bae, Cpx, Psp, Rcs, and σ<sup>E</sup> pathways and showed that these pathways can be activated simultaneously in response to exogenous or endogenous stimulation, while natural environmental stimuli provoke bacterial modifications that lead to multiple pathway responses |CITS:[19763168]|. 
Each of these responses might be specialized to ensure a specific aspect of envelope biogenesis and maintenance; on the other hand, CpxR could have a role as a modulator of the response by integrating other endogenous signals. Bury-Moré et al. concluded that all five pathways are needed to mount a full response to extracytoplasmic stress |CITS:[19763168]|.

name: pspF
note:
An integrated transcriptomic study of the extracytoplasmic stress response, which detects and responds to alterations of the bacterial envelope, was performed by Bury-Moré et al. (2009). They investigated the interconnections and dynamics between Bae, Cpx, Psp, Rcs, and σ<sup>E</sup> pathways and showed that these pathways can be activated simultaneously in response to exogenous or endogenous stimulation, while natural environmental stimuli provoke bacterial modifications that lead to multiple pathway responses |CITS:[19763168]|. 
Each of these responses might be specialized to ensure a specific aspect of envelope biogenesis and maintenance; on the other hand, CpxR could have a role as a modulator of the response by integrating other endogenous signals. Bury-Moré et al. concluded that all five pathways are needed to mount a full response to extracytoplasmic stress |CITS:[19763168]|.

name: cusCFBA
note: cusCFBA expression is induced under Zn excess in a CusR-dependent way |CITS: [31586033] |. This transcription unit may even include the pheP gene that is downstream of the cusA gene. cusB expression is increased at least 5-fold by indole |CITS: [15686558]|. The mRNA that contains the cusA gene has been observed mainly in the cellular membrane |CITS: [31540875]|.

name: inaA
note: Salicylate activates the inaAp and micFp promoters through Rob |CITS:[22753060]|.

name: iscRSUA
note: Expression of the iscRSUA operon is induced by hydrogen peroxide and paraquat |CITS: [11443091]|. An iscS mutant affects the expression of udp (UPase), cdd (CDA), and yeiT-yeiA (DPD). Based on the study mentioned above, Mihara et al. (2008) postulated that an unknown factor, depending on the Fe-S cluster, controls either directly or indirectly the expression of these genes |CITS:[18482579]|. The iscS gene plays an important role in the expression of pyrimidine metabolism genes and provides proof of the potential relationship between iscS and global gene regulation |CITS:[18482579]|. Under either anaerobic or aerobic growth conditions, IscR represses transcription of the operon iscRSUA, which encodes genes for the Fe-S cluster biogenesis pathways. The repression of the isc operon by IscR responds to the demand for the Fe-S cluster in the medium, because IscR has to be bound to an Fe-S group to be able to repress the transcription of the isc operon |CITS:[23075318]|. Under anaerobiosis, the demand for the Fe-S group is lower than under aerobiosis, and therefore the repression of the operon is stronger than under aerobiosis |CITS:[23075318]|. The accumulation of the protein IscR is enhanced during iron-limiting conditions, while the other proteins encoded in the iscRSUA operon are diminished; these findings are in agreement with the fact that IscR is a repressor of the operon |CITS: [31235512]|. IscR recognizes and binds two DNA-binding sites that overlap the promoter sequence to repress transcription of the iscRSUA operon. Therefore, it appears that when the protein is bound to these sites, the RNA polymerase is not able to bind to the promoter region to start transcription |CITS:[23075318]|. Although the two sites are required for complete repression of the operon, the binding of IclR to one of them does not depend on the presence of the other |CITS:[23075318]|. IscR regulates in a coordinated manner the expression of the iscRSUA to the sufABCDSE operons through repression by [2Fe-2S]-IscR and activation by apo-IscR, respectively. Both the apo- and holoprotein conformations were able to activate the Suf pathway in order to maintain differential control for Fe-S cluster biogenesis pathways to ensure viability under a variety of growth conditions |CITS:[25266384] |.

name: gabDTP
note: This transcription unit is part of a complex operon that contains three promoters, two of which are internal. Although |CITS: [12446648]| et al. suggested that the csiR gene is part of the gabDTP operon because they saw a polar effect of an insertion in gabT on gabC, |CITS: [14731280]| et al. saw that the presence or absence of the gabD promoters did not affect the csiR expression and therefore concluded that the csiR gene is transcribed in another transcription unit. For this reason, this gene was not included in the operon. The gabDTP operon is induced in response to nitrogen starvation |CITS:[30498244]|.

name: osmY
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for osmY . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: oppA
note: Another TSS was identified upstream of the oppAp2 promoter, but the sequence reported in the paper |CITS: [36860088]| does not coincide with the sequence of that in strain E. coli K-12 MG1655.

name: proV
note: The transcript of this transcription unit appears to terminate approximately 13 nucleotides downstream from the predicted stem-loop structures located after the proV gene |CITS: [37978176]|.

name: proW
note: The transcript of this transcription unit appears to terminate approximately 5 nucleotides downstream from the predicted stem-loop structures located after the proW gene.
