name: nmpC
note: Based on microarray and RT-qPCR analyses, it was determined that the yebF and nmpC genes are up- and downregulated, respectively, upon exposure to glutathione (GSH) |CITS:[29468195]|.

name: cydAB
note: The cydAB operon is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|. The expression of the gene cydB is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression under aerobiosis appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: cmk-rpsA
note: Under anaerobiosis, FNR represses rpsA gene expression, but it is not known which promoter (cmk, rpsAp1, or rpsAp3) is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|.

name: napFDAGHBC-ccmABCDEFGH
note: Based on ChIP-chip and consensus sequences (Virtual Footprint), a site of NsrR upstream of the napF gene was identified |CITS:[19656291]|. However, this gene exerts its control from three promoters, and it is not known which of them is negatively controlled by NsrR.

name: gadBC
note: |CITS:[10383761]| and |CITS:[8455549]| have reported evidence of a negative effect of Hns protein on the gadBC operon. However, |CITS:[15795232]| demonstrated that Hns does not bind the regulatory region of the operon. Therefore, the HNS effect on gadBC is indirect. Genes involved in the gad (gadABCDEWX) system are common under many adverse conditions, as determined by microarray analyses and Fourier transform-infrared spectroscopy. Although they are known to be important for the acid stress response, it has also been shown that part of this system is also upregulated by NaCl, cold stress, ethanol, and heat stress |CITS:[19767843]|. Basal GadE activity is required for activation of gadA and gadBC expression during stationary-phase growth |CITS:[17185552]|. The gadBC operon is upregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|. The transcription of gadB appears to be increased under acidic growth conditions during the exponential phase in a RcsB-dependent manner, but not during stationary phase |CITS:[21571995]|. gadB is induced during biofilm formation |CITS:[15795232][12657059]|. RcsB activity through both the RcsCD phosphorelay pathway and the RcsA pathway lowers the acid resistance |CITS:[17185552]|. The role of this negative regulation might be to prevent costly runaway expression of the gad genes or to shut off the response, once the acid stress is over |CITS:[17185552]|. Indole enhances the expression of several genes related to acid resistance, such as gadA, gadB, gadC, hdeA, hdeB, hdeD, slp, and gadE |CITS:[20470880]|. The acid resistance phenotype induced by indoles is mainly due to increased expression of the glutamine decarboxylase system |CITS:[20470880]|. The mdtEF-tolC, malEFG, gadBC, treBC, entCEBA-ybdB, hdeAB-yhiD, and alaE operons were differentially regulated after induction of the nonnative organic acid citramalate |CITS:[31186337]|. CsrA regulates the expression of gadB under mildly acidic conditions and neutral conditions during the stationary phase |CITS:[38319100]|.

name: trpCBA
note: FNR represses trpB gene expression under anaerobiosis, but it is still not known which of the two promoters (trpCp or trpLp) is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|.

name: glpEGR
note: An increase in the expression of glpE has been observed when CysB is highly overexpressed |CITS: [31823602]|.

name: cmr
note: The expression of the cmr gene is affected by antibiotic pressure. An increase in cmr expression is maintained during exposition to increasing concentrations of tetracycline |CITS:[17426813]|.

name: acpP-fabF
note: This transcription unit is part of a group of genes (encoding acyl carrier protein and several key fatty acid biosynthetic enzymes) which are not transcribed as a classic operon, but rather multiple promoters are present and each gene is cotranscribed with at least one other gene |CITS: [8655562]|.

name: fabDG-acpP
note: This transcription unit is part of a group of genes (encoding acyl carrier protein and several key fatty acid biosynthetic enzymes) which are not transcribed as a classic operon, but rather multiple promoters are present and each gene is cotranscribed with at least one other gene.

name: fabG-acpP
note: This transcription unit is part of a group of genes (encoding acyl carrier protein and several key fatty acid biosynthetic enzymes) which are not transcribed as a classic operon, but rather multiple promoters are present and each gene is cotranscribed with at least one other gene.

name: dadAX
note: The transcript of dadAX is decreased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|. dadAX operon expression is upregulated upon L-valine addition (isoleucine starvation) in the cellular growth medium |CITS: [32211022]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for dadA . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: dadAX
note: dadAX operon expression is upregulated upon L-valine addition (isoleucine starvation) in the cellular growth medium |CITS: [32211022]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for dadA. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: garPLRK-rnpB
note: Because there is no obvious terminator between garK and rnpB genes, transcription from the upstream promoter, garPp, might very well be expected to produce a polycistronic transcript that ends at the rnpB terminator.

name: rnc-era-recO-pdxJ-acpS
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rnc. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: cfa
note: Both promoters cfap2 (σ38) and cfap1 (σ38 and σ70) are induced in response to neutral acetate |CITS:[16420371][18331469]|, and they do not need accessory elements (activators or repressors), which suggests that acetate works directly with RNA polymerase in the transcription complex. The general responses to acetate of the two RNA polymerase forms were clearly different; σ70 promoters responded with wide differences, but σ38 promoters were generally activated |CITS:[18331469]|. cfa transcription is also induced by acidic and osmotic stress |CITS:[11244055][15716429]|. cfap1 contains a perfect 14-bp inverted repeat with two half-site elements that read 5'-TTTTGTT-3', centered at position -40. No information concerning this inverted repeat is available, since it does not align with any known regulator-binding sites |CITS:[18331469]|.

name: cfa
note: Both promoters cfap2 (σ38) and cfap1 (σ38 and σ70) are induced in response to neutral acetate |CITS:[16420371][18331469]|, and they do not need accessory elements (activators or repressors), which suggests that acetate works directly with RNA polymerase in the transcription complex. The general responses to acetate of the two RNA polymerase forms were clearly different; σ70 promoters responded with wide differences, but σ38 promoters were generally activated |CITS:[18331469]|. cfa transcription is also induced by acidic and osmotic stress |CITS:[11244055][15716429]|. cfap1 contains a perfect 14-bp inverted repeat with two half-site elements that read 5'-TTTTGTT-3', centered at position -40. No information concerning this inverted repeat is available, since it does not align with any known regulator-binding sites |CITS:[18331469]|.

name: relA-mazEF
note: Based on microarray analysis, Traxler et al. (2008) determined that relA positively regulates its own expression |CITS:[18430135]|. The control exercised by relA, on glycogen levels may result from an interplay of its (p)ppGpp-mediated positive effect on glgCAP transcription, deviation of surplus ATP flux from nucleic acid and protein biosynthetic pathways to glycogen production, and its negative effect on intracellular levels of the GlgC negative effector AMP |CITS:[19702577]|.

name: mazEFG
note: Based on deletion of rpoS, it has been shown that the stationary-phase sigma factor σs is responsible for resistance to chpRA-mazG-mediated cell death during the stationary growth phase |CITS:[19251848]|.

name: mazEFG
note: Based on deletion of rpoS, it has been shown that the stationary-phase sigma factor σs is responsible for resistance to chpRA-mazG-mediated cell death during the stationary growth phase |CITS:[19251848]|.

name: ilvLXG_1G_2MEDA
note: The ilvLGMEDA operon, which codes for four of the five enzymes required for isoleucine and valine biosynthesis, is primarily regulated by attenuation in response to the abundance of the end products of those pathways |CITS: [6252193]|. It is also regulated by transcriptional regulators IHF and Lrp.

name: ilvLXG_1G_2MEDA
note: The ilvLGMEDA operon, which codes for four of the five enzymes required for isoleucine and valine biosynthesis, is primarily regulated by attenuation in response to the abundance of the end products of those pathways |CITS: [6252193]|. It is also regulated by transcriptional regulators IHF and Lrp.

name: ilvEDA
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for ilvE . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: poxB
note: The poxB expression is reduced when the cell is grown on acetate, pyruvate, or glycerol |CITS: [33417680]|.

name: xthA
note: With expression analysis, it was demonstrated that the gene xthA is not regulated by the transcription factors Fur, IscR, and SoxS, nor is it regulated by the sigma factor RpoS |CITS: [34942039]|

name: rnpB
note: Fis activates rnpB transcription most effectively at moderate salt concentrations |CITS: [15879709]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for rnpB. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: adhE
note: A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for adhE . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: pqiABC
note: The pqiABC operon is related to the Mla transport pathway and is important for membrane integrity |CITS: [27795327]|.

name: rpsA-ihfB
note: During exponential growth himD expression is mainly directed by the rpsAp1 or rpsAp3 promoter, while in the stationary phase the himD promoter is primarily responsible for himD activity. Under anaerobiosis, FNR represses rpsA gene expression, but it is not known which promoter (cmk, rpsAp1, or rpsAp3) is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|. A possible role in the rpsA-ihfB, adhP, fecIR, and fecABCDE operons was described for functional abortive RNAs (faRNAs), based on computational prediction and described by Nair et al. 2017 |CITS:[28347827]|.

name: rpsA-ihfB
note: During exponential growth himD expression is mainly directed by the rpsAp1 or rpsAp3 promoter, while in the stationary phase the himD promoter is primarily responsible for himD activity. Under anaerobiosis, FNR represses rpsA gene expression, but it is not known which promoter (cmk, rpsAp1, or rpsAp3) is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|. A possible role in the rpsA-ihfB, adhP, fecIR, and fecABCDE operons was described for functional abortive RNAs (faRNAs), based on computational prediction and described by Nair et al. 2017 |CITS:[28347827]|.

name: hlyE
note: SlyA activates hlyE trasnscription by antagonizing the negative action of the H-NS protein |CITS: [10027972], [14996792], [32849447]|. H-NS prevents binding of RNA polymerase to the hlyE promoter. On the other hand, SlyA allows binding of RNA polymerase but inhibits binding of H-NS |CITS: [17892462]|. The location of the sites for SlyA reported by |CITS: [17892462]| are unusual for typical bacterial transcription. However, it has been shown that some other S. typhimurium genes that are positively regulated by SlyA contain SlyA-binding sites downstream from the transcriptional start site, including pagC and ugtL |CITS: [15813739], [15208313]|. FNR and CRP are in competition for binding at the hlyE regulatory region, and FNR ocuppies the site more frequently than CRP even under aerobic conditions, but under such conditions FNR is not an active protein and so this protein cannot activate trancription of hlyE as efficiently as CRP. The expression of hlyE CRP-mediated activation requires the presence of Hns.

name: degP
note: The expression of the degP gene, encoding a protease, is affected by tetracycline antibiotic pressure. As tetracycline concentration increases, the gene expression also increases. The role of the induced protease could be degradation of porins to protect the cell against the antibiotic |CITS:[17426813]|. The CpxR-dependent gene expression of degP is increased under the stress condition caused by the introduction of the R1-16 plasmid, which encodes the type IV secretion system (T4S machinery) in the cell |CITS:[16952953]|.

name: gadAX
note: Genes involved in the gad (gadABCDEWX) system are common under many adverse conditions, as determined by microarray analyses and Fourier transform-infrared spectroscopy. Although they are known to be important for the acid stress response, it has also been shown that part of this system is also upregulated by NaCl, cold stress, ethanol, and heat stress |CITS:[19767843]|. Basal GadE activity is required for activation of gadA and gadBC expression during stationary-phase growth |CITS:[17185552]|. The transcription of gadAX appears to be increased under acidic growth conditions during stationary and exponential phases in a RcsB-dependent manner |CITS:[21571995][23274360]|. RcsB activity through both the RcsCD phosphorelay pathway and the RcsA pathway lowers the acid resistance |CITS:[17185552]|. The role of this negative regulation might be to prevent costly runaway expression of the gad genes or to shut off the response, once the acid stress is over |CITS:[17185552]|. Indole enhances the expression of several genes related to acid resistance, such as gadA, gadB, gadC, hdeA, hdeB, hdeD, slp, and gadE |CITS:[20470880]|. The acid resistance phenotype induced by indoles is mainly due to increased expression of the glutamine decarboxylase system |CITS:[20470880]|. The gadAp promoter is strongly induced by GreA overproduction only when DksA is absent, as are many other genes |CITS: [22056927]|. gadA is induced during biofilm formation |CITS:[15795232][12657059]|. CsrA regulates the expression of gadA under mildly acidic conditions |CITS:[38319100]|.

name: glnK-amtB
note: The transcription of the glnK-amtB operon is regulated by nitrogen availability; while nitrogen limitation causes operon expression, nitrogen excess represses completely the operon expression |CITS:[16740928]|. Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the glnK gene increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, under nitrogen-limited growth conditions, gene expression was decreased |CITS:[21272324]|.

name: yiaKLMNO-lyx-sgbHUE
note: Under aerobic conditions, expression of the yiaKMNO operon, together with the ula regulon, is necessary for l-ascorbate assimilation. This compound induces transcription of the operon at the end of the exponential phase and at the beginning of the stationary phase.

name: dnaAN-recF
note: The autoregulation of dnaA is growth temperature dependent |CITS:[23092251]|.

name: paaABCDEFGHIJK
note: Some, but not all, E. coli K-12 strains lack the ability to utilize phenylacetate; E. coli strains MG1655 and W exhibit utilization, although E. coli C does not exhibit utilization due to deletion of a large fragment that includes the paa region |CITS: [9748275]|.

name: yceD-rpmF
note: FNR represses yceD gene expression under anaerobiosis, but it is not known which of the two promoters transcribing the gene is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|.

name: yceD-rpmF
note: FNR represses yceD gene expression under anaerobiosis, but it is not known which of the two promoters transcribing the gene is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|.

name: aroP
note: The aroPp2 promoter is partially repressed by TyrR alone; this repression is enhanced in the presence of tyrosine or phenylalanine |CITS:[10515932]|. A lesser effect of repression of the aroP gene by tryptophan has also been observed |CITS:[3025182]|. For the repression of aroPp2, an interaction between the N-terminal domain of TyrR and the RNA polymerase is required |CITS:[10515932]|. In the presence of tyrosine and ATP, the TyrR hexamer is bound to two tyrR boxes, a weak and a strong box located 43.5 and 20.5 bp downstream of the aroPp2 promoter, respectively. At these positions, TyrR represses the aroP gene expression, disabling the formation of the open complex |CITS:[10515932]|. In the presence of phenylalanine or in the absence of an effector, the TyrR dimer bound to the strong TryR box represses the transcription by inhibiting the step(s) that follows the formation of the open complex |CITS:[10515932]|.

name: nac
note: The transcription of the nac gene is induced under nitrogen-limiting conditions |CITS: [9785451]|. Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the nac gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur |CITS:[21272324]|. It has been observed that the transcriptional regulator IHF binds near the start gene of nac |CITS: [34475859]|.

name: phoPQ
note: The expression of the gene phoP is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. phoP expression is also induced under low-Mg2+ conditions and to a greater extent by the antimicrobial peptide C18G, in a PhoP-dependent manner |CITS:[27471053]|.

name: hmp
note: Membrillo et al. in 1998 showed that MetR at low concentrations activates the hmp gene, when it binds to the binding site located at -80 |CITS: [9767577]|, and simultaneously represses transcription of the glyA gene |CITS: [9767577]|. In addition, they showed that homocysteine did not alter the levels of transcription of this gene and that S-nitrosoglutathione and sodium nitroprusside increase transcription of the hmp gene in the presence of MetR. Based on gene expression analysis, it was shown that CsgD could be a negative regulator of hmp expression |CITS:[23824318]|.

name: hyfABCDEFGHIJR-focB
note: This operon is apparently silent, because it was not expressed at a significant level in a wild-type strain under the tested conditions |CITS: [9387241]|.

name: proVWX
note: By making use of microarray analysis it was concluded that FNR represses proVWX operon expression under anaerobiosis. A putative FNR-binding site, which was not reported in the paper, was identified upstream of this operon, but it is still not known which of the three promoters (proUp1, proUp2, or proUp3) is affected by FNR |CITS: [12754220]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for proW . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: proVWX
note: In addition to the coregulation of the proVWX operon, each of the genes has a degree of independent regulation. The transcription of this operon is repressed by H-NS filaments and is induced under osmotic shock |CITS: [37978176]|. The expression response of the proVWX operon under osmotic stress involves changes in its three-dimensional structure mediated by H-NS |CITS: [37978176]|. Based on several analyses, in |CITS: [37978176]|, a model is proposed for the regulation of the proVWX operon by H-NS. Under low osmolarity conditions, the H-NS sites for DNA binding and dimerization are exposed (available) to bind DNA, forming interactions between some DNA sequences of the operon. This structure interferes with RNA polymerase binding. Upon osmotic shock, the cell imports K+, destabilizing the H-NS structure, weakening or interrupting the interactions between regions of the operon DNA. The promoter becomes exposed for activation, and transcription proceeds by disrupting the interactions. The cellular response is the export of K+, which allows H-NS to bind again and repress the operon. When the cell adapts to osmotic stress, H-NS regulation is diminished. RNAse III processes the 3' end of the operon, both under osmotic shock and during adaptation to osmotic stress |CITS: [37978176]|. Using microarray analysis it was concluded that FNR represses proVWX operon expression under anaerobiosis. A putative FNR-binding site, which was not reported in the paper, was identified upstream of this operon, but it is still not known which of the three promoters (proUp1, proUp2, or proUp3) is affected by FNR |CITS: [12754220]|. IHF is a trans-acting factor implicated directly in the regulation of the proVWX operon |CITS:[25673659]|. IHF helps to relieve H-NS repression of the proVWX operon in an off state rather than by acting as an activation factor |CITS:[25673659]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for proW. This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: proVWX
note: By making use of microarray analysis it was concluded that FNR represses proVWX operon expression under anaerobiosis. A putative FNR-binding site, which was not reported in the paper, was identified upstream of this operon, but it is still not known which of the three promoters (proUp1, proUp2, or proUp3) is affected by FNR |CITS: [12754220]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for proW . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: grxA
note: The transcription of the gene grxA is enhanced under high oxygen saturation (300%) in the absence of the superoxide dismutase proteins SodA and SodB |CITS:[ 23497217]|. Sulfane sulfur modifies OxyR at Cys199 to form a protein persulfide that in turn activates the expression of grxA, trxC, and katG promoters under both aerobic and anaerobic conditions |CITS:[31421411]|. The transcript of grxA is increased after σE induction, as observed in high-throughput analysis of gene expression |CITS: [31992590]|.

name: mutS
note: Transcription of the mutS gene might be negatively regulated by σ38 through an intermediate regulator, as was shown in |CITS [9393714]|. On other hand Hfq negatively regulates mutS expression, destabilizing its transcription during exponential growth. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for mutS . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: zraP
note: The transcription of zraP is induced by high concentrations of zinc |CITS: [21674266]|.

name: rsd
note: Based on analysis using a promoter-specific transcription factor screening system (PS-TF), it has been shown that the rsd and rmf promoters are repressed by ArcA and activated by McbR, RcdA, SdiA, and SlyA transcription factors (TFs) during the transition from exponential to stationary growth phase. On the other hand, a total of 11 TFs (CueR, DmlR, GlpR, QseF, OmpR, UxuR, BtsR, YfeR, YhaJ, YiuA, and YijO) were estimated to bind to the rmf promoter |CITS:[ 30225374]|. The SlyA-binding site could be overlapping the ArcA-binding site in rsd in the upstream region of the promoter. For the other three TFs (McbR, RcdA, and SdiA), their wide binding sequences are between approximately bp -100 and -200 for McbR, between -120 and -220 for RcdA, and between -50 and -280 for SdiA upstream of the start of the gene |CITS:[ 30225374]|. The TFs are controlled by specific effector ligands which decrease in the presence of the corresponding effector, such as AcP for ArcA and AI-1 for SdiA, and increase in its presence, such as AI-2 for McbR, acetate for RcdA, and ppGpp for SlyA |CITS:[ 30225374]|.

name: pfkA
note: Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the pfkA gene was increased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, it is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on gene expression; also, it is unknown which of the two promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene pfkA is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression of this gene is in agreement with the increased specific glucose consumption rate observed under acidic conditions |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: mlc
note: mlc expression is enhanced under anaerobic conditions |CITS:[11535784]|. Under nitrogen-rich growth conditions, the expression of the dgsA gene was decreased in mutants for two genes that encode two terminal oxidases, cyoA and cydB, and in mutants for two transcriptional regulators, Fnr and Fur. However, it is unknown if the effects of the transcriptional regulators act directly on the gene expression ; also, it is unknown which of the two promoters that transcribe the gene could be regulated by the regulators |CITS:[21272324]|. The expression of the gene mlc is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression under aerobiosis appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|, but it is not known which promoter, of two transcribing mlc, is affected by PhoB.

name: mlc-ynfK
note: mlc expression is enhanced under anaerobic conditions |CITS:[11535784]|. The expression of the gene mlc is increased under acidic growth conditions in either aerobiosis or microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression under aerobiosis appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|, but it is not known which promoter, of two transcribing mlc, is affected by PhoB.

name: acnA
note: The expresion of the acnA gene is induced under oxidative stress, although this induction is not early after the stress is generated |CITS: [12889026]|. SoxS is a regulator that participates in controlling several genes involved in the response to oxidative stress. SoxS facilitates the binding of RNA polymerase to some promoters, including the acnA promoter, during low iron availability |CITS [11470373]|. The acnA gene is downregulated by long-term (8 to 12 h) exposure of E. coli to some biocides |CITS: [32385082]|.

name: acnA
note: The expresion of the acnA gene is induced under oxidative stress, although this induction is not early after the stress is generated |CITS: [12889026]|. SoxS is a regulator that participates in controlling several genes involved in the response to oxidative stress. SoxS facilitates the binding of RNA polymerase to some promoters, including the acnA promoter, during low iron availability |CITS [11470373]|.

name: acnB
note: The expression of acnB is enhanced during and after the switch from aerobic to anaerobic growth conditions |CITS:[ 21980479]|. A potential RNA G-quadruplex structure, formed by guanine-rich sequences located in the coding sequence region of the gene, was identified for acnB . This structure could regulate the expression of the gene, as observed for hemL gene expression |CITS: [31964733]|.

name: paaXY
note: Based on the improved computational program DTI (Direct Information criterion), which is an extension of the concept of MI that allows investigators to measure the direction of information flow between two random variables, and DTInfer, a new R-package for the inference of gene regulatory networks from microarrays based on directed information, the paaY gene was identified as a new target of PaaX based on a high Z-score |CITS:[20718955]|. The paaABCDEFGHIJK and paaXY operons might be cotranscribed |CITS:[20718955]|.

name: yjeFE-amiB-mutL-miaA-hfq-hflXKC
note: This superoperon contains a complicated arrangement of alternating σ<sup>32</sup> promoters and σ<sup>70</sup> promoters.

name: yjeFE-amiB-mutL-miaA-hfq-hflXKC
note: This superoperon contains a complicated arrangement of alternating σ<sup>32</sup> promoters and σ<sup>70</sup> promoters.

name: cdd
note:
Gavigan et al. (1999) and Perini et al. (1996) showed that CytR binds in tandem in the regulated intergenic region |CITS:[10347150] [8969182]|. On the other hand, in 1997, Pedersen and Valentin-Hanses showed that CytR binds to octamer repeats, GTTGCATT in either the direct or inverted orientation and preferably separated by 2 or 3 bp |CITS: [9155036][9466254]|. For this reason the length of the CytR DNA-binding site is variable.
However, footprinting analyses showed that the dimers of CytR are flanked or "sandwiched" by two dimers of CRP |CITS:[20000490][1327747][9466254]|. Thus, the binding sites of CytR located in this region were assigned by the curator in agreement based on similarity to the consensus sequence and on the data from the footprinting assays and mutational evidence |CITS:[9466254][1327747][20000490][9155036][2170326][12391883]|.
The repressor CytR and the activator CRP, two dimeric proteins, interact to form a complex repressor nucleoprotein in the intergenic region. When only CRP is bound to this promoter, it functions as an activator, and then, when CytR binds to DNA and to CRP, the activation is repressed because CytR masks an activating region of CRP that otherwise would contact the RNA polymerase to activate transcription |CITS:[1327747][ 8736525][10766824]|. The CytR protein cannot act alone; the synergistic DNA binding is increased by direct interaction with CRP |CITS:[1327747][1962841][ 2170326][1649947]|. At times CytR also repositions CRP to alternative DNA-binding sites that are not functional for activation |CITS:[ 8736525] [1327747]|.

name: phoH
note: The expression of the gene phoH is increased under acidic growth conditions in aerobiosis but not in microaerobiosis |CITS:[23274360]|. The increased expression appears to be caused by the transcription factor PhoB |CITS:[23274360]|.

name: yajC-secDF
note: The yajC gene is expressed at about 5- to 10-fold higher levels than either secD or secF. FNR represses yajC gene expression under anaerobiosis, but it is not known which of the two promoters transcribing the gene is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|.

name: tgt-yajC
note: CFNR represses yajC gene expression under anaerobiosis, but it is not known which of the two promoters transcribing the gene is affected, or even if this regulation is direct or indirect |CITS: [12754220]|.

name: aroKB-damX-dam-rpe-gph-trpS
note: In addition to the localization of a consensus sequence to bind the transcription factor CpxR upstream of aroK, the expression of the gene is increased by CpxR |CITS:[ 11953442]|, but it is not known which promoter, of two transcribing the gene, is affected by CpxR.
